          The estimated cost of this report for the Department of the Navy (DON) is
          $56,902. The estimated total cost for supporting the DON budget justification
OPN 1
          material is approximately $3,113,561 during the 2025 fiscal year. This includes
          $202,708 in supplies and $2,910,853 in labor.
          The estimated cost of this report for the Department of the Navy (DON) is
          $98,398. The estimated total cost for supporting the DON budget justification
OPN 2
          material is approximately $3,113,561 during the 2025 fiscal year. This includes
          $202,708 in supplies and $2,910,853 in labor.
          The estimated cost of this report for the Department of the Navy (DON) is
          $22,577. The estimated total cost for supporting the DON budget justification
OPN 3
          material is approximately $3,113,561 during the 2025 fiscal year. This includes
          $202,708 in supplies and $2,910,853 in labor.
          The estimated cost of this report for the Department of the Navy (DON) is
          $37,623. The estimated total cost for supporting the DON budget justification
OPN 4
          material is approximately $3,113,561 during the 2025 fiscal year. This includes
          $202,708 in supplies and $2,910,853 in labor.
          The estimated cost of this report for the Department of the Navy (DON) is
          $28,617. The estimated total cost for supporting the DON budget justification
OPN 5-8
          material is approximately $3,113,561 during the 2025 fiscal year. This includes

              UNCLASSIFIED

                                     Volume 1 - vi
                         Department of Defense Appropriations Act, 2026

Other Procurement, Navy

For procurement, production, and modernization of support equipment and materials not
otherwise provided for, Navy ordnance (except ordnance for new aircraft, new ships, and ships
authorized for conversion); the purchase of passenger motor vehicles for replacement only;
expansion of public and private plants, including the land necessary therefore, and such lands and
interests therein, may be acquired, and construction prosecuted thereon prior to approval of title;
and procurement and installation of equipment, appliances, and machine tools in public and
private plants; reserve plant and Government and contractor-owned equipment layaway,
$17,904,970,000 to remain available for obligation until September 30, 2028, of which $1,076,000
shall be available for the Navy Reserve and the Marine Corps Reserve: Provided, That such funds
are also available for the maintenance, repair, and modernization of ships.


Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 Overseas Operations Costs funding accounted for in the Base budget total
$5,050,000.

 Description:
 Surface Power Equipment budget line provides life-cycle support for the following Marine Gas Turbine systems:

 The General Electric LM2500 Marine Gas Turbine provides main propulsion for the Navy's surface combatants including the CG 47 TICONDEROGA Class, DDG 51 ARLEIGH BURKE Class, and LCS-2 (Austal)
 Class. The LM2500 is composed of two major sub-assemblies: the gas generator and power turbine sections. It is coupled to the ship drive-train by a high speed coupling shaft. The LM2500+ Marine Gas
 Turbine and its associated Engineering Control Systems provide main propulsion power for the Navy's amphibious assault ships including the LHD-8 USS MAKIN ISLAND, LHA-6 USS AMERICA and LHA-7 USS
 TRIPOLI. The LM2500+ is composed of two major sub-assemblies: the gas generator and power turbine sections. It is coupled to the ship drive-train by a high speed coupling shaft. The control system for both
 engines provides for both local and remote engine operations.

 The Rolls Royce (Allison) 501-K Series Gas Turbines are used to drive electrical generators in Ship Service Gas Turbine Generators (SSGTG). The 501-K17 is used on the CG-47 Class ships. The 501-K34 is
 an upgraded version used on the DDG-51 Class ships and is not interchangeable with the 501-K17. The MT5S is a higher-power derivative of the 501-K engine family (utilizing Rolls Royce's new "Marine Trent"
 or "MT" naming convention vice the older "501-K" naming convention) used for auxiliary electrical generation on DDG-1000 class ships; the MT5S is not interchangeable with the 501-K17 or 501-K34. The MT5S
 is used within the RR4500 auxiliary turbine generator (ATG) aboard DDG-1000 Class ships. The MT5S-HE+ is used within the AG9160RF SSGTG aboard DDG-51 Flight III ships. Based on the MT5S design,
 the MT5S-HE+ includes several material changes designed to improve fuel efficiency and increase AG9160RF electrical power output when compared to the RR4500. The Rolls Royce MT-30 is used on LCS-1
 (Lockheed) Class for main propulsion and DDG 1000 for electrical generation. Rolls Royce 250-KS4 is used as a starter gas turbine for the 501-K34 on DDG 79 and follow. The Rolls Royce MT7 gas turbine is
 used for both lift and propulsion fan power on the next generation of LCAC landing craft.

 Digital Fuel Control (DFC) procurement quantities are based upon the DDG modernization schedule and quantities are procured one year in advance of the scheduled availabilities due to long material lead
 times.

 The Surface Power Equipment budget line may also be used to procure spare waterborne shafting (propeller shafts and stern tube shafts) and controllable pitch propeller (CPP) system end items such as
 propeller blades, hubs and oil distribution (OD) boxes used on DDG 51 class ships.

 [P40A / PE009 GE Readiness Improvement Upgrade Kits]: PE009 - MODIFICATION KIT PROGRAM: This cost element procures modification kits listed below for the GE Gas Turbine Engine. These
 modifications increase reliability and fleet readiness through component upgrades which improves the Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF). The modification kits are procured based on historical usage rates, re-

Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 packaged into individual kits, and sent to either a depot to be installed during engine overhauls or to Regional Maintenance Centers for installation. Procurement quantities are not shown because these funds are
 used to procure a variety of modification kits, with specific kit quantities and prices varying from one year to the next. The kits included in the cost code are as follows:
 --Accelerometers (Vibration Monitor)
 -- Compressor Rear Frame Mid-flange Fastener installation
 -- C-Sump Lubrication Supply Line
 -- Power Turbine Upgraded Speed Sensor
 -- Improved Drain Shutoff Valve
 -- Turbine Mid-Frame Modification
 -- 7B Piping Modification
 -- 5-Element to 6-Element Pump Conversion
 -- T5.4 Flexible Thermo Harness
 -- Mid Flange Fastener

 [P40A / PE010 Stock Rotating Spares]: PE010 - GAS GENERATOR: This cost element procures the LM2500 and LM2500+ single shank propulsion gas turbines to support surface combatant and amphibious
 assault class surface ships. The programs projected in-service population of single shank propulsion gas turbine engines is estimated to be 382 due to new construction. Breakdown of ship class spare
 procurement is listed below. The procurement of these engines includes all required shipping/storage containers.

 DDG LM2500 Single Shank
 Based on experience with 35 years of usage demand data on the LM2500 Engine and IAW 10 USC Sec. 2308, DFAR subpart 207.70 and DoDI 3110.06, a total of 36 spares are required to support the fleet
 requirements.

 LCS 2 LM2500 Single Shank.
 Based on experience with 35 years of usage demand data on the LM2500 Engine and IAW 10 USC Sec. 2308, DFAR subpart 207.70 and DoDI 3110.06, a total of 3 spares are required to support the fleet
 requirements. As of FY21 no requirement to procure LM2500 Single Shanks.

 LHD8/LHA6/LHA7 LM2500+ Single Shank Gas Generator Assembly (SST GGA) and Power Turbine Assembly (PTA)
 Based on experience with 35 years of usage demand data on the LM2500 Engine and IAW 10 USC Sec. 2308, DFAR subpart 207.70 and DoDI 3110.06, a total of 2 spares are required to support the fleet
 requirements. Based on sparing requirement procurement of 2 additional LM2500+ engines are required. Total of 4 engines are required.

 LM2500 Hot Section Kits
 Are procured as required from the below list per quarterly failure analysis and maintained to replace High Pressure Turbine (HPT) components beyond repairable limits. Shortages of replacement blades increase
 engine repair turn-around time (RTAT) during engine refurbishment/repair as a result of material shortages.

 Procurement quantities are a result of procuring various kits of various costs. The kits included in the cost code are as follows:
 --HPT Shrouds
 --HPT Seal Assembly
 --HPT Blades
 --HPT Nozzles
 --Compressor Blades

 [P40A / PE014 Special Support Equipage]: PE014 Special Support Equipage - This cost element procures Special Support Equipment for all marine gas turbines to support gas turbine changeouts and to allow
 for increased I-level (intermediate level) repair capability, thereby reducing the number of engine change outs required and stabilizing or reducing the cost to overhaul engines at the depot. No installation cost is
 associated with this cost element.

 [P40A / PE015 LM2500 DIGITAL FUEL CONTROL]: PE015 - LM2500 DIGITAL FUEL CONTROL (DFC)- This cost element procures the Digital Fuel Control (DFC) shipsets, ancillary equipment and install
 material to support the CG/DDG modernization schedules. Procurement and installations are budgeted to correspond with CNO ship availabilities. One DFC kit is required to update each of the four LM2500
 engines, which makes one DFC shipset. The DFC upgrade is a mandatory portion of DDG-51 modernizations; the modernized machinery control system (MCS)is incapable of interfacing with LM2500 engines
 not equipped with DFC. The DFC is the most significant change to the LM2500 engine since its inception into the USN that addresses obsolete components which accounted for approximately 40% of all engine
 failures. In summary, this modification is estimated to be 75% more reliable than the old system. This upgrade also replaces the Hydro-mechanical main fuel control (MFC) system, which was designed for
 aircraft, with a DFC system designed for a military marine environment. This modification increases engine reliability, decreases periodic maintenance, enhances operational functions, and provides an automatic
 stall detection and shutdown capability.

 PE6IN - INSTALLATION FOR DIGITAL FUEL CONTROL (DFC)- This cost element funds the installation of Cost element PE015, LM2500 Digital Fuel Control (DFC) shipsets, as part of the CG/DDG HM&E
 modernization program. This includes pre-assembly at NSWCPD (Philadelphia), shipping to installation location, shipboard installation, training, post installation operational testing and associated travel cost.

 [P40A - 2 / PE001 STOCK ROTATING SPARES]: PE001 - STOCK ROTATING SPARES
 This cost element procures the MT-30 for DDG1000 Zumwalt Class and Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) Independence Variant, MT-5 series gas turbines for the DDG 1000 Zumwalt Class and DDG-51 Flight III
 program, the 250-KS4 SSGTG starter engines for the DDG 79 and follow, and the 501-K34 gas turbines DDG 51 class ships. The procurement of these engines includes all required shipping/storage containers
 and necessary hardware to allow for installation of the MT30 gas turbine into DDG1000 or LCS5AF hull types.

 MT-30 Gas Turbines
 Based on experience with similar turbines and IAW 10 USC Sec. 2308, DFAR subpart 207.70 and DoDI 3110.06, a total of 6 spares are required to support the fleet.

 MT-5 Gas Turbines
 Based on experience with similar turbines and IAW 10 USC Sec. 2308, DFAR subpart 207.70 and DoDI 3110.06, a total of 4 MT-5 spares are required to support the fleet. The in-service population of MT-5S gas
 turbine engines is six (6) due to new construction of DDG1000 and thirty (30) of the higher efficiency MT5S-HE+ units for DDG-51 Flight III.

 250-KS4 SSGTG Starter Engines
 Based on experience with similar turbines and IAW 10 USC Sec. 2308, DFAR subpart 207.70 and DoDI 3110.06, a total of 12 spares are required to support the fleet requirements. Current spares on-hand
 is twelve (12) which achieves the baseline sparing requirement. The projected FY-19 in-service population of starter engines is 103 due to new construction, but would be 169 if Flight IIA and Flight III were
 included. Operation Tomodachi has resulted in the radiological contamination of twelve (12) 250-KS4 Marine Gas Turbines, which created an unplanned requirement to replace the twelve (12) affected engines
 beginning in FY13. Completion of procurement of 12 replacement engines in FY23.

 501-K34 Gas Turbines
 Based on experience with similar turbines and IAW 10 USC Sec. 2308, DFAR subpart 207.70 and DoDI 3110.06, a total of 26 spares are required to support the fleet requirements.

 [P40A - 2 / PE007 MODIFICATION PROGRAM]: PE007 - MODIFICATION PROGRAM
 This cost element procures modification kits listed below for the Rolls Royce Marine Gas Turbines (MGT). Analysis of Rolls Royce MGT engineering performance data, metrics, the SEA 21 Top Tech Issues and
 the component improvement program has identified necessary improvements to correct Rolls Royce MGT deficiencies. The modifications addresses the Fleet's top maintenance and reliability issues by reducing
 failure rates of system components, and improve Rolls Royce MGT readiness.

 The modification kits are packaged and shipped to the ship for installation during schedule CNO availability by a team of NSWCPD (Philadelphia) and Alteration Installation Team (AIT) personnel. Procurement
 quantities are not shown because these funds are used to procure a variety of modification kits, with specific kit quantities and prices varying from one year to the next. The kits included in the cost code are as

 [P40A - 3 / PE830 PRODUCTION ENGINEERING]: PE830 PRODUCTION ENGINEERING The review and approval of any production contract technical documentation, or the separate development of this
 documentation to include:
 - Technical Manuals
 - Signal Flow Diagrams
 - PMS
 - Level III Production Drawings
 - Provisioning Technical Documentation (PTD)
 - Program Support Data
 - Allowance Parts Lists (APL's)
 - Engineering in support of final design reviews.

 This includes efforts associated with tracking and updating Navy Data Environment (NDE), Marine Gas Turbine Information System (MGTIS) and Weblog to reflect completed installations and ensure consistent
 documentation for engine configuration management.

 [P40A - 4 / PE110 Propellers and Shafts]: PE110- VARIOUS PROPELLERS AND SHAFTS - This funding procures controllable pitch propeller (CPP) system end items such as propeller blades, hubs and
 oil distribution (OD) boxes used on DDG 51, LHD 8 and following (AF), LPD, LHA 6 AF, LSD 41, and DDG1000 class ships. These spares are required to DDG 51, LHD 8 and following (AF), LPD, LHA 6 AF,
 LSD 41, and DDG1000 Fleet demand due to scheduled replacement caused by lifecycle wear and unscheduled replacements caused by Casualty Reports (CASREPs). The component has long repair turn-
 around times (1-2 years) and thus cannot be repaired and reused during the same availability. The technical requirements and drydocking constraints drives the program to establish a rotatable pool to support
 replacements. The growing size of the fleet and insufficiency of the current number of DDG 51, LHD 8 and following (AF), LPD, LHA 6 AF, LSD 41, and DDG1000 spares to meet upcoming change-out demand
 per CNO availability schedules is driving the requirement to increase the number of spares to support a rotatable pool and ensure readiness. A malfunctioning propeller or shaft may result in excessive vibration,
 which could lead to damage of ship structures and equipment (bearings, main reduction gears, and bulkhead seal), increase of ship cavitation/noise, loss of speed or loss of motion.

 [P40A - 5 / PE130 - Steam Propulsion Items]: PE130 - STEAM PROPULSION ITEMS - This provides for several initiatives oriented to upgrading boiler efficiency and safety with downstream maintenance
 effectiveness. In particular, the items procured include: Upgraded Stack Gas Analyzer (SGA) , Optimization Trim Loop Material including fuel oil meters, stack gas temperature sensors, electronic automatic boiler
 controls interface software/schema to improve steam plant efficiency, Upgraded electronic automatic boiler control (E-ABC) system process controllers/smart transmitters, Upgraded Boiler Water/Feedwater
 (BW/FW) Chemistry Analysis/Carbohydrazide Implementation Equipment., Upgraded SSTG Protective Devices (Overspeed Trips), and Upgrades to Boiler Igniter System (BIS) (Fuel Valve and Insert/Retract
 Air Actuators). The Steam Propulsion Improvement Program provides safe and reliable power for ship movement through the water, and, in addition, provides power to ships combat and habitability systems,
 whether electrical or steam dependent. At any given time, due to propulsion plant casualties, ship propulsion systems may be operating at reduced capability, adversely affecting the ship's mission(s). The Steam
 Propulsion improvement program encompasses steam and diesel propulsion surface ships in the Fleet, and provides for material upgrades to propulsion systems resulting in increased readiness, safety and
 reliability. Items can be installed during a scheduled or unscheduled availability by a shipyard, tender/Intermediate Maintenance Activity or Alteration Installation Team (AIT).

 Justification:
 The decrease in FY 2026 funding is due to the changes to the Full Authority Digital Control (FADC) procurement and installation schedule. FY 2026 funding of $9.978M is required to support procurement
 of DDG51 Gas Turbine Generator Engine Controller procurements and installations, procurement of non-nuclear surface ship controllable pitch propeller assets, and lesser cost gas turbine parts for depot
 overhaul kits. Procurements are scheduled to ensure on time delivery of assets for dry dock availabilities, combat obsolescence of specific parts, and maintain up to date configuration of all engines going through
 overhauls.

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $.009 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 Footnotes:
 (3)
       The decrease in FY 2026 is due to procurement schedule of KS4.
 (4)

 (5)
    PE007 MODIFICATION PROGRAM: The FY 2026 funding increase is based on procurement/ship availability schedules and funding0 to support XT FADC controller procurement. The modification program is designed to procure and
 install hardware to affect improvements in reliability and maintainability aboard CG-47 and DDG-51 class Ship Service Gas Turbine Generator (SSGTG) units. Each hull has a total of three (3) SSGTG units installed. Each SSGTG is
 controlled by a Full Authority Digital Control (FADC). CG-47 class uses the 501-K17 and DDG-51 class uses the 501-K34. There are currently three variants of the 501-K34 SSGTG: AG9130, AG9140HF and AG9140RF. The AG9140RF
 also includes the Remote Independent Mechanical Starting System (RIMSS) consisting of the 250-KS4 engine. The quantity of modification kits procured corresponds to the number of SSGTG units to be upgraded. The type and scope of
 each modification required will vary from one ship to the next. The number and type of kits installed in any given Fiscal Year (FY) varies depending upon ship availability schedules during that FY.

 Description:
 PE015 - LM2500 DIGITAL FUEL CONTROL (DFC) - This cost code procures the Digital Fuel Control (DFC) shipsets, ancillary equipment and install material to support the CG/DDG modernization schedules.
 Procurement and installations are budgeted to correspond with CNO ship availabilities. One DFC kit is required to update each of the four LM2500 engines, which makes one DFC shipset. The DFC upgrade is a
 mandatory portion of DDG-51 modernizations; the modernized machinery control system (MCS)is incapable of interfacing with LM2500 engines not equipped with DFC. The DFC is the most significant change to
 the LM2500 engine since its inception into the USN that addresses obsolete components which accounted for approximately 40% of all engine failures. In summary, this modification is estimated to be 75% more
 reliable than the old system. This upgrade also replaces the Hydro-mechanical main fuel control (MFC) system, which was designed for aircraft, with a DFC system designed for a military marine environment.
 This modification increases engine reliability, decreases periodic maintenance, enhances operational functions, and provides an automatic stall detection and shutdown capability.

 PE6IN - INSTALLATION FOR DIGITAL FUEL CONTROL (DFC)- This cost element funds the installation of Cost element PE015, LM2500 Digital Fuel Control (DFC) shipsets, as part of the CG/DDG HM&E
 modernization program. This includes pre-assembly at NSWCPD (Philadelphia), shipping to installation location, shipboard installation, training, post installation operational testing and associated travel cost.

Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                 -              -               -              -              -               -              -               -         -            -    -            -


 Description:
 This program allows the Navy to better align and identify Hull, Mechanical & Electrical (HM&E) requirements and shipboard upgrades and equipment. This is in support of the in-service Surface Combatants
 and Amphibious ships to include Class Common Equipment (CCE) to support shorter Regular Overhauls and Selected Restricted Availabilities. These include Ship System Components that require long repair
 turn-around; CG/DDG - Machinery Control Systems (MCS) Commercial off the Shelf (COTS) Refresh; Ship Control Systems (SCS) procurements and installation in support of the SCS; and other equipment to
 support the Comprehensive Review Findings following the Fitzgerald and McCain collisions in 2017.

 [P40A / GB001 - CG/DDG COTS Tech Refresh]: Due to Diminishing Manufacturing Sources and Material Shortages (DMSMS), Commercial Off The Shelf (COTS) equipment must be periodically refreshed to
 ensure supportability to the Fleet. This cost code is used to fund solutions for HM&E systems that have parts with impending DMSMS impacts. DMSMS solutions, including after-market manufacturing (reverse
 engineering), bridge-buys, life-of-type buys, modernization through replacement, modification / redesign, and reclamation are implemented to ensure that in-service ships are operational and sustainable
 throughout the life of the system. DMSMS issues and planned resolutions are implemented both within and outside of standard CNO availabilities (Window of Opportunity (WOO) or Continuous Maintenance
 Availability (CMAV)) and are impacted by pending fact-of-life influences, including shifting availabilities, modified vendor End of Sale dates, and unforeseen material shortages.

 [P40A / GB002 - Class Common Equipment]: The Common Class Equipment (CCE) program is to provide Ready-For-Issue (RFI) systems and equipment in support of Surface Combatant ship industrial
 availabilities and to replace major system equipment which sustains battle damage or major failure. CCE Availability Assets (AAs) ensure warfighting capability, readiness, and on-time deployment by providing
 RFI long lead time material (LLTM) in case of possible catastrophic damage or major failure on Aegis ships. CCE AAs are procured and stored to support backfit upgrades and emergent maintenance within brief
 availability windows. The ready accessibility of these assets avoids delays in availability completion caused by lengthy equipment repair turn-around time or acquisition lead time. In addition to AAs, the CCE
 program also provides for Insurance Assets. These are the major long lead time HM&E equipment/systems, which would prevent a ship from being able to be quickly repaired in order to accomplish its mission
 due to major damage or battle damage. The CCE program provides for both CG and DDG assets.

 [P40A / GB003 - Ship Control Systems (Surface Ship Modernization)]: Funds provide support for the procurement and installation of Ship Control Systems (SCS) upgrades on both cruisers and destroyers in
 support of the Fleet Modernization Program. Ship Control Systems provide lifecycle support for legacy and modernized engineering controls. SCS equipment and components are procured for various ship
 changes and engineering change proposals for in-service surface combatants and are installed both within and outside of standard CNO availabilities. This effort includes maintaining Land Based Engineering
 Sites (LBES), CG / DDG system refresh, SCD design engineering, CG / DDG equipment procurement, and CG / DDG ISEA/ILS support. This cost code also provides for funding the necessary software licenses
 in support of the HM&E COTS systems/equipment for both CG and DDG class ships. Examples of systems requiring software licenses are: Machinery Control System (MCS) components, Voyage Management
 System, Integrated Bridge System, Integrated Bridge and Navigation System, and Digital Video Surveillance System.

 [P40A / GB6IN - DDG 51 Class Install (Procured under GB003 3.2)]: Install

 FY26: 2; FY27: 4; FY28: 2; FY29: 3

 [P40A / GB004 - Maritime Energy Initiatives (Previously Procured in BLI 0110 and BLI 0120)]: Maritime Energy Initiatives provide surface combatants with technologies/tools that reduce energy usage. This will
 enhance operational capabilities, allowing for longer time on-station and fewer refuelings. The initiatives consist of: 1) Hull and Hydrodynamics - These initiatives focus on the dynamic forces where the ship
 contacts the water. They include, but are not limited to hull appendages that reduce drag, thus improving speed and fuel efficiency; 2) Power Generation and Transmission Systems - These initiatives focus on,
 but are not limited to improving the efficiency of ships' prime movers, generators, main reduction gears, propulsion motors/drives, and shaft-line components; 3) Combat and Ship Systems Support Services -
 These initiatives focus on, but are not limited to improving systems that use electricity when providing support services (e.g. cooling, heating, air, lube oil) to combat machinery, or life support systems; and 4)
 Decision Aids - These initiatives will inform the sailor of decisions they can make to save energy and improve operational efficiency by consolidating and manipulating ship data and providing it back as actionable
 information.

 [P40A / GB004 - Shipboard Energy Dashboard FLT IIA Equipment]: The Shipboard Energy Dashboard FLT IIA technology is a shipboard tool providing real time situational awareness of the energy demand
 associated with equipment lineup and mission. Technology raises operator awareness of how certain engineering plant lineups and equipment affect fuel consumption rates. Energy savings will be realized
 through energy usage culture change.

 [P40A / GB004 - Maritime Energy Initiatives (VSD for CPS)]: The Collective Protective System (CPS) is a full time air filtration system integrated into the ship's HVAC system. The amount of outside supply air
 delivered to CPS zones varies with filter loading over time and is almost always higher than needed, resulting in above average HVAC loads. Variable Speed Drive (VSD) will be installed as an airflow control
 component to optimize supply air flow, realizing energy savings from fan motor and reduced HVAC loads. Currently, no funds required in FY26 and out years.

 [P40A / GB004 - Maritime Energy Initiatives (TMCS)]: The Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system is the largest distributed system on a Surface Combatant and typically consumes between
 25% and 40% of all energy used. Surveys on several DDG-class ships indicate that average space temperatures across the ship were being maintained well below the design temperature. Thermal
 Management Control System (TMCS) provides centralized control and monitoring of space temperatures at design set-points for each compartment throughout the ship. This technology enables improved
 operational efficiency of the HVAC system, thereby lowering AC loads and reducing electrical demand. Currently, no funds required in FY26 and out years.

 [P40A / GB004 - Maritime Energy Initiative Install (Procured prior to transfer)]: Currently, no funds required in FY26 and out years.

 [P40A / GB005 - HM&E Condition System]: This solution proposed would implement HM&E principles, it would implement a Defense-in-Depth Functional Implementation Architecture (DFIA) and meet the
 requirements of the standards released by the Information Assurance Technical Authority Board (IA TAB) across all the HM&E systems deploying the ability to protect, detect, react, and restore in a contested
 environment. The HM&E Condition System will monitor the signals on the HM&E network, both between HM&E systems and also those coming from other functional domains.

 [P40A / GB006 - Hybrid Electric Drive]: Currently, no funds required in FY26 and out years.

 [P40A / GB443 - Bridge Integration - Ship Control Systems (BI-SCS)]: In accordance with OPNAV N9 Top Level Requirements (TLR) for Surface Ship Bridge Standardization and Integration (9MAR2020), funding
 provides solutions to 2018 Comprehensive Review (CR) recommendations 7.3.6, 7.3.7, 7.3.10, and 7.3.11 for DDG-51 ship control and bridge information systems as identified by NAVSEA's DDG-51 Class
 Design Review Board (DRB) across five major areas of focus including: 1) Adding a physical throttle to the Integrated Bridge and Navigation System (IBNS) Ship Control Console (SCC); 2) Adding a Voyage
 Data Recorder (VDR) capability; 3) Implementing night vision requirements for lighting and glare reduction from bridge equipment; 4) Replacing troubled Original Equipment Manufacturing (OEM) developed
 Steering and Propulsion Control with Ship Control System-Government owned (SCS-GOV) Software; 5) Implementing a Bridge Lighting and Display strategy to enhance display visibility during day and nighttime
 operations, as well as maintain warfighter night vision during night operations. Additionally, in accordance with OPNAV N9 TLR and CR recommendations for AMPHIBIOUS Ship Classes - funding was provided
 to add Voyage Data Recorder (VDR) Capability.

 [P40A / DDG-51 Flight III Unique System Battle Spares]: GB008 DDG-51 Flight III Unique System Battle Spares. The Battle Spare procurement is to provide Fleet Commanders the ability to repair major system
 equipment unique to the Flight III ship configuration in the event of battle damage or major failure. Procurements support long lead-time HM&E systems that would not otherwise be available to support quick
 repairs to operational ships. These systems include but are not limited to: Power Control Monitors (PCMs), Ship Service Gas Turbine Generators (SSGTGs), Water Mist, High Efficiency Super Capacity (HES-C),
 and 4160 Switchboards and Load centers.

 Justification:
 The $15.588M decrease between FY25 and FY26 is due to Maritime Energy Initiative not occurring in FY26 and reduced efforts on Bridge Integration - Ship Control System Combatant Voyage Data Recorders
 and Physical Throttles.

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $3.195 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 Footnotes:
 (1)
     Procurements in cost code GB003 are dependent upon the specific alterations to be accomplished and an individual ship's configuration. Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within a
 fiscal year. Quantity of "1" represents the total value of multiple shipsets purchased on multiple contracts for multiple ships. Unit costs for specific alterations are not associated with cost code GB003 due to the varying degree of engineering
 changes required per ship (ship configuration dependent) and the installation normally occurs outside of CNO availabilities, in either a WOO or CMAV.
 (2)
       GB003 Scheduled Procurements for DDG 51 Class (To be installed under GB6IN 5.2):                      Procurement    FY26: 2
 (3)
    The increase between FY24 and FY25 for Cost Code GB004 adds funds to procure lighting upgrades for DDG-51 and the MSC fleet, develop ship energy optimization projects, investigate future combatant hull design, and set up a pilot
 program to test commercial energy technologies on Navy ships.
 (4)
    GB443 - The increase between FY25 and FY26 captures a funded Issue for non-recurring engineering, material procurements and installations of selected modifications aligning Integrated Bridge Network Systems (IBNS) configurations
 with new DDG Ship Control Systems (SCS) configurations to address obsolescence, cyber concerns, and configuration management. Additionally, Cost Elements were added to provide traceability, clarity, and reportability on the separate
 products that support Bridge Integration - Ship Control Systems for both Combatant and Amphibious ship classes.
 (5)
       Funds shortfalls to planned procurements and installations of SCS GOV due to increase in contract pricing and new requirement for Synchro System Feedback Module (SSM) hardware in IBSN-configured DDGs.

 Description:
 This program provides procurement and improvements of navigation equipment to include Inertial Navigation equipment such as gyrocompasses, speed sensors, Electronic Chart Display and Information System
 (ECDIS) and major components for other navigation systems. ECDIS provides Fleet-wide electronic charting capability, increases navigation and situational awareness, improves safety at sea, and eliminates
 reliance on paper charts. These systems provide mission critical navigation data to shipboard combat and weapon systems.

 [P40A / GW054: NAVIGATION TRAINING EQUIPMENT]: Funds are required for the procurement of training equipment for multiple Navigation classrooms in support of Navigation System Technician pipeline
 maintenance training and ECDIS Operator Training. This includes computer upgrades and software licenses. These upgrades are critical to maintaining Fleet proficiency on operation and maintenance of
 navigation systems. Field Changes include ECDIS Operator Course Classroom Tech Refresh and additional seats and licenses and upgrade/update Mariner Skills Simulator (MSS) to support Fleet Training
 Requirements (Radar/Data bases/Models).

 [P40A / GW063: NAVIGATION CERTIFICATION]: These funds are required for conducting Navigation Certification (NAVCERT) on surface ships, submarines, and carriers as part of fielded upgrades and tech
 refresh. NAVCERT funding amount varies each fiscal year based on the number and type of equipment and/or field changes installed, the ship class, and location.

 [P40A / GW065: NAVIGATION SHORESITE EQUIPMENT]: These funds are required for the procurement and Non Recurring Engineering (NRE)/ Integrated Logistics Support (ILS)/Support of navigation system
 shoresite equipment. Shoresite equipment does not have any associated installation costs.

 [P40A / GW066: SSBN NAVIGATION SENSOR OBSOLESCENCE]: These funds are required for Procurement and NRE/ILS/Support of replacement Navigation Sensors on SSBN Platforms. Transition from
 P40A to P3A in FY25 to reflect additional procurement and associated installations.

 [P40A / GW068: NAVIGATION CERTIFICATION SUITE]: These funds are required for the procurement of hardware and software to maintain and upgrade a land-based Navigation Certification Suite lab to verify
 navigation equipment field changes and tech refresh interoperability with existing systems prior to shipboard installation.

 [P40A / GW830: PRODUCTION ENGINEERING]: These funds are required for the production engineering support of all navigation system hardware, software, and tech refresh procurements and system
 test and integration. This Cost Element includes indirect costs associated with the procurement and installation of individual field changes and upgrades. These costs would include field change and upgrade

                                                                                                                                                                                                    Volume 1 - 25

Navigation Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                     Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 support, consolidated engineering support, Program Office support, and other costs. These costs will fluctuate with increases and decreases of overall budget, number and type of field changes and upgrades,
 and required support.

 [P40A / GW Completed P3As]: NAVSSI Capstone (GW058) - Capture Prior Year Historical totals for completed P3A effort. See prior submissions for details on this completed P3A effort
 Next Generation Fathometer (GW060) - Capture Prior Year Historical totals for completed P3A effort. See prior submissions for details on this completed P3A effort

 [P3A / GW053: AN/WSN-12]: These funds are for the procurement and installation of AN/WSN-12 Inertial Navigation System - Replacement (INS-R) on surface ships, submarines, and carriers.

 [P3A - 2 / GW055: SPEED LOGS]: These funds are for the procurement and installation of Speed Log upgrades and Tech Refresh on surface ships, submarines, and carriers.

 [P3A - 3 / GW056: NAVY ECDIS]: These funds are required for the software build, procurement, and installation of Navy ECDIS hardware and software to replace legacy ECDIS-N capability on surface ships,
 carriers, and submarines (software only).

 [P3A - 4 / GW061: MK 27 REPLACEMENT]: These funds are required for the procurement and installation of AN/WSN-11 replacement for legacy MK27 Backup Heading Reference systems on surface ships
 and submarines.

 [P3A - 5 / GW062: AN/WSN-7 MODERNIZATION]: These funds are required for the procurement and installation of field changes, upgrades, and Tech Refresh of AN/WSN-7 Inertial Navigation System on
 surface ships, submarines, and carriers.

 [P3A - 6 / GW069: SITUATIONAL AWARENESS BOUNDARY ENFORCEMENT AND RESPONSE - NAVIGATION (SABER-NAV)]: Divestment of ($41.7M) for ANCHOR prototype due to higher DON priorities.
 This Cost Element was previously labeled as ANCHOR. These funds are required for the procurement and installation of Situational Awareness Boundary Enforcement and Response-Navigation (SABER-NAV)
 system. SABER-NAV is the common codified solution that provides cyber situational awareness suite, intrusion detection/prevention, boundary defense, cross-domain solutions (CDS), and various host-level data
 protections.

 [P3A - 7 / GW070: MILITARY GLOBAL POSITIONING SYSTEM (GPS) USER EQUIPMENT (MGUE)]: This Cost Element reflects a transition from development(PE 0204228N) to production in FY 2023. These
 funds are required for the procurement and installation of Military GPS User Equipment on submarines.

 [P3A - 8 / GW071: NAVIGATION NETWORKS]: GW071 title change from Navigation Critical Distribution System to Navigation Networks to incorporate NCDS and NDDS. This Cost Element was added in PB22
 cycle with funding and program responsibility transferred from LI 0946 (NAVIGATION DATA DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM (NDDS) NETWORK INSTALLATION) and LI 0933 (Navigation Critical Data Distribution
 (NavCrit)) to LI 0670 starting in FY23. These funds are required for the procurement and installation of Navigation Critical Distribution System (NCDS) and Navigation Data Distribution System (NDDS) on
 amphibious ships and carriers.

 [P3A - 9 / GW072: AUTOMATED CELESTIAL NAVIGATION SYSTEM]: This Cost Element reflects a transition from development(PE 0204228N) to production in FY 2023. These funds are required for the
 procurement and installation of Automated Celestial Navigation System (ACNS) on surface ships.

 [P3A - 10 / GW073: TIME FREQUENCY DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM-REPLACEMENT]: TIME FREQUENCY DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM-REPLACEMENT]: This Cost Element reflects a transition from development
 (PE 0204228N) to production in FY 2025. These funds are required for the procurement and installation of Time Frequency Distribution System-Replacement (TFDS-R) on submarines.

 [P3A - 11 / GW066: SSBN NAVIGATION SENSOR OBSOLESCENCE]: Transition from P40A to P3A in FY25 to reflect additional procurement and associated installations which will address obsolescence issues
 associated with the AN/BSN-2 Digital Depth Detector and TFDS. It also leverages updates to COLUMBIA Class architecture to remove dedicated Mission Critical Control Panel (MCCP).

 Justification:
 Net decrease of $.261M from FY 2025 to FY 2026 reflects decreases in planned procurement and installation requirements for NCDS, AN/WSN-12 and Production Engineering.

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $7.477 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 Footnotes:
 (1)
       SURFACE NAVIGATION TRAINING EQUIPMENT: The cost increase from FY 2025 to FY 2026 by greater than 2% due to additional manpower requirements for surface navigation training equipment.
 (2)
       AMPHIB NAVIGATION CERTIFICATION: Decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 due to fewer navigation certification events expected based on navigation fielding plans that fluctuate from year to year.
 (3)
       SURFACE NAVIGATION CERTIFICATION: Decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 due to fewer navigation certification events expected based on navigation fielding plans that fluctuate from year to year.
 (4)
       SUBMARINE NAVIGATION CERTIFICATION: Decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 due to fewer navigation certification events expected based on navigation fielding plans that fluctuate from year to year.
 (5)
       CARRIER NAVIGATION CERTIFICATION: Decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 due to fewer navigation certification events expected based on navigation fielding plans that fluctuate from year to year.
 (6)
       SURFACE OSS AND COURSE REPEATERS: Funding decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 due to no additional requirements for surface OSS and course repeaters.
 (7)
       SURFACE NAVIGATION CERTIFICATION SUITE: Decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 due to decrease in procurement and engineering support requirements.
 (8)
       AMPHIB PRODUCTION ENGINEERING (Navigation Networks): Decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 is based on the required engineering support for Navigation Networks in FY 2026.
 (9)
       AMPHIB PRODUCTION ENGINEERING: Decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 is based on the required engineering support for AMPHIB modernization efforts in FY 2026.
 (10)
        SURFACE PRODUCTION ENGINEERING: Decreases are based on Surface navigation system fielding requirements in FY 2026.
 (11)
        SUBMARINE PRODUCTION ENGINEERING: Decreases are based on Submarine navigation system fielding requirements in FY 2026.
 (12)
        CARRIER PRODUCTION ENGINEERING: Decreases are based on Carrier navigation system fielding requirements and Navy ECDIS funding in FY 2026.

Method of Implementation (Organic): NAVY ECDIS SOFTWARE PRODUCTION - Not Installed                                                        Installation Quantity: 0



 Footnotes:
 (29)
    NAVY ECDIS HARDWARE: Navy ECDIS Hardware funds are required for the procurement of a next generation Scalable Integrated Bridge System (SIBS) design as well as a new SIBS design based on
 DDG-class Integrated Bridge Navigation System (IBNS) for surface ships and land-based facilities. Navy ECDIS Hardware costs decrease from FY 2025 to FY 2026 is based on the system delivery requirements.
 Costs reflected in exhibit 1.1.2 are not for procurement of actual Navy ECDIS HW but to fund associated activities for the procurement.
 (30)
     NAVY ECDIS SOFTWARE PRODUCTION: Funding is for initial Navy ECDIS software production build and follow-on Navy ECDIS software builds on a two-year cycle. Costs decrease from FY25 to FY26
 due to requirement changes.
 (31)
        AMPHIB NAVY ECDIS HARDWARE: Procurement cost increase from FY25 to FY26 is due to a higher unit cost of new Navy ECDIS HW (SIBS GEN3) than the previous hardware suite.
 (32)
    AMPHIB NAVY ECDIS SOFTWARE: Quantity reflects number of hulls to be upgraded. Planned bulk license procurement as part of Navy ECDIS Software Production costs and are outlined in (CE 2.1.1).
 Governments owns the Navy ECDIS SW license, and therefore, there's no separate procurement cost for Navy ECDIS SW. Navy ECDIS software has no production lead-time.
 (33)
        SURFACE NAVY ECDIS HARDWARE: Procurement cost increase from FY25 to FY26 is due to a higher unit cost of new Navy ECDIS HW (SIBS GEN3) than the previous hardware suite.
 (34)
    SURFACE NAVY ECDIS SOFTWARE: Quantity reflects number of hulls to be upgraded. Planned bulk license procurement as part of Navy ECDIS Software Production costs and are outlined in (CE 2.1.1).
 Governments owns the Navy ECDIS SW license, and therefore, there's no separate procurement cost for Navy ECDIS SW. Navy ECDIS software has no production lead-time.
 (35)
        CARRIER NAVY ECDIS HARDWARE: Procurement cost increase from FY25 to FY26 is due to a higher unit cost of new Navy ECDIS HW (SIBS GEN3) than the previous hardware suite.
 (36)
    CARRIER NAVY ECDIS SOFTWARE: Quantity reflects number of hulls to be upgraded. Planned bulk license procurement as part of Navy ECDIS Software Production costs and are outlined in (CE 2.1.1).
 Governments owns the Navy ECDIS SW license, and therefore, there's no separate procurement cost for Navy ECDIS SW. Navy ECDIS software has no production lead-time.
 (37)
        SUBMARINE NAVY ECDIS SOFTWARE SUSTAINMENT: Beginning in FY25 this line item addresses funding requirements for submarine specific navigation and ISEA efforts. This is not a new start.
 (38)
     SURFACE NAVY ECDIS SOFTWARE INSTALLATION: Starting in FY25 contract date has moved to March of each FY. There is no contract delay. Contract award and delivery timeline was shifted to better
 align with the latest installation plan.
 (39)
     CARRIER NAVY ECDIS HARDWARE: Procurement of SIBS GEN3 hardware suite to start in FY24 with installation funding required 18 months prior to installations due to AIT contract lead time. SIBS GEN3
 hardware suite and prior configurations lead time is 12 months. There's no contract delay. Contract award and delivery timeline was shifted to better align with the latest installation plan.
 (40)
     SURFACE NAVY ECDIS HARDWARE: Procurement of SIBS GEN3 hardware suite to start in FY24 with installation funding required 18 months prior to installations due to AIT contract lead time. SIBS GEN3
 hardware suite and prior configurations lead time is 12 months. Starting in FY26, a 24-month production lead time is required as a result to a new Navy ECDIS HW (NAV C1) that will be procured and fielded.

 align with the latest installation plan.

 Description:
 GW071 title change from Navigation Critical Distribution System to Navigation Networks to incorporate NCDS and NDDS. This Cost Element was added in PB22 cycle with funding and program responsibility
 transferred from LI 0946 (NAVIGATION DATA DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM (NDDS) NETWORK INSTALLATION) and LI 0933 (Navigation Critical Data Distribution (NavCrit)) to LI 0670 starting in FY23. These
 funds are required for the procurement and installation of Navigation Critical Distribution System (NCDS) and Navigation Data Distribution System (NDDS) on amphibious ships and carriers.

 (62)
     For installations, funding is required one year in advance due to AIT contract lead-time. NDDS manufacturers: Lyme Computing Solutions for Siemens, Raytheon (San Diego, CA), and Brandywine

      This is not a new start. This Cost Element was created to reflect funding and program responsibility transferal due to centralization of Program Acquisition Resource Manager (PARM) for navigation systems

 (65)
        PLT is 12 months, install funding required at least one year prior to planned installations. FY24 install funding covers three installs in FY25 and two installs in FY26.

 (66)
     Procurements did not occur in FY23 due to OPNAV directed design changes that significantly increased production lead time. Procurement and installation quantities have been reduced in FY25 and beyond

 latest labor costs and the lessons learned from the first shipboard installation. Procurement quantity increase from FY 2025 to FY 2026 is based on the system delivery requirements.

Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                               -          46.657          46.565         28.865             -           28.865             -               -         -            -     -            -
Flyaway Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                             -              -               -              -              -               -              -               -         -            -     -            -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                 -              -               -              -              -               -              -               -         -            -     -            -


 Description:
 The FY 2026 request for SUB PERISCOPE, IMAGING AND SUPT EQUIP PROG includes $135,863 thousand of discretionary and $141,550 thousand of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $277,413
 thousand. The mandatory funds procure, support, and install Integrated Submarine Imaging System (ISIS) and Electronic Warfare (EW) BLQ-10 systems. These systems are part of the Submarine Warfare
 Federated Tactical Systems (SWFTS), a family of interdependent systems that conduct cooperative system development, procurement, and installations.

 The Submarine Periscope, Imaging, and Support Equipment program provides critical electromagnetic systems to all classes of submarine platforms. This includes Imaging, Electronic Warfare (EW), and
 RADAR systems which provide navigation safety and collision avoidance, particularly in high intensity littoral operations so that surveillance and weapons systems can be effectively employed. Integrated
 Submarine Imaging System (ISIS) and EW BLQ-10 systems are part of the Submarine Warfare Federated Tactical Systems (SWFTS), a family of interdependent systems that conduct cooperative system
 development, procurement, and installations. PMS435 procures and installs ISIS and EW BLQ-10 Technical Insertion (TI) shipsets, along with the other SWFTS Participating Acquisition Resource Managers
 (PARMs), PMS401 (A-RCI) under BLI 2150 and PMS425 (BYG-1) under BLI 5420. SWFTS TI shipsets must be procured and installed in like quantities (except EW SSBN (OHIO)) across all PARMs to maintain
 the SWFTS model.

 The Submarine Periscopes and Imaging Equipment Program procures ISIS including Type 18 and Type 8 periscope upgrades; the Photonics Mast and its variants, including the Type 20 and Type 24; inboard
 hardware kits; imaging upgrades for all submarine classes; Photonics land-based spares, depot tooling and mast maintenance equipment. ISIS supports high intensity operations in the littorals, providing the
 submarine force with the tactical imaging systems necessary to safely and effectively employ its surveillance and weapons capabilities. The infrared imaging capability provided by ISIS provides all weather
 imaging capability, improving imaging at night and in low visibility conditions. Technology Insertion initiatives include the common control and displays hardware and software on all platforms regardless of
 imaging sensors, an integrated imaging system that provides for operator alerts, imaging enhancement tools and contact analysis tools, incorporation of Type 20 Mast, Type 24 Mast, future sensors, and a fully
 Submarine Warfare Federated Tactical System (SWFTS) integrated system. Funding improves submarine imaging capability in the areas of: ship safety, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (ISR),
 and tactical control (contact management in the littorals) by providing high quality imaging 24 hours a day, 7 days a week in all weather conditions to support submarine operations worldwide.

 By OPNAV Ltr Ser. N77/3U629209, 12 June 2003, OPNAV N87 established the ISIS to rapidly field these systems and integrate existing periscope imagery systems into a single system for installation on board
 submarines. An ISIS Capability Development Document (CDD) was approved 22 Dec 2011, with an update to the CDD approved on 15 Mar 2018 and a CDD Annex for Low Profile Digital Photonics Mast
 approved on 02 Dec 2019. The CDD is used to fully integrate the ISIS program of record into the submarine force's rapid technology insertion process and to incorporate Fleet-endorsed requirements such as
 Low Profile Photonics Mast (LPPM).

ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                        Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                  Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 PL010 - Imaging ISIS Technical Insertion
 Continues procurement and engineering of Technical Insertion kits, Design Services Allocation (DSA) installation support, and spares to upgrade ISIS systems on LOS ANGELES, SEAWOLF, VIRGINIA, OHIO
 SSGN/SSBN, and COLUMBIA Class SSBN platforms to allow for capability insertion and obsolescence avoidance. Starting with TI-16, funding includes the one time change for improved copper-fiber optic
 diploops and hull penetrators (Mast Compatibility) required to support advanced Imaging and early warning capabilities included in LPPM, Type 20, and Type 24 Masts.

 PL011 - Imaging Upgrades: Type 8/Type 18
 Continues procurement of Periscopes and Imaging Equipment reliability, capability, and maintainability upgrades, e.g., Eyepiece Box, periscope mechanical hoisting mechanism upgrades, inboard electronic
 processing hardware and associated Integrated Logistics Support (ILS), Robust Surveillance Program, Certification of regional repair centers for Imaging System maintenance and technical data. Variable
 quantities and types are bought in each fiscal year based on Fleet-endorsed requirements. Procures Infrared (IR) Collision Avoidance Systems for the SSBN (OHIO) Class submarines.

 PL012 - Periscope Special Support Equipment
 Procures replacements in support of Special Support Equipment (SSE) for each maintenance level to ensure imaging systems are maintained in a state of operational readiness. Equipment includes Q-Band
 Test Equipment, Mast Dynamic Collimator, Eyebox/Mast Test Set, and Antenna/Outer Head Simulator required due to obsolescence of existing imaging systems SSE.

 PL015 - Periscope Interim Contractor Support
 Funding is for Interim Contract Support provided by the periscope manufacturer. The majority of the funding is for Type 18 service life extension, depot repair and upgrades for all configurations of Photonics
 Masts including LPPM, Type 20, and Type 24 masts. It also funds all levels of repairs for all types of tactical submarine imaging systems and establishes the Photonics National Maintenance Center (NMC) to
 accelerate availability and delivery of photonics masts to the fleet. FY25 begins to provide Low Profile Mast (LPM) special support equipment (SSE) and special test equipment (STE) to increase the LPM repair
 capability at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii to support critical U.S. Pacific Fleet (PACFLT) operations.

 PL016 - Periscope Training
 Funding is for imaging systems training requirements to include curriculum development, training materials, and initial factory training pilot course conduct, Navy Training Plans, and instructor advisory services.

 PL025 - Low Profile Mast
 Funding is for the procurement, support and technical insertion of VIRGINIA and COLUMBIA Class low profile imaging masts (including LPPM, Type 20, Type 24, and Type 2S) production modules and land
 based spare masts and associated components. The spares will be used for replacement Imaging Masts on VIRGINIA and COLUMBIA Class submarines. Quantity of spares will increase with VIRGINIA and
 COLUMBIA submarine procurements. Funding is also for Mast Capability Enhancements (MCE) and System for Non-Acoustics Control of Signatures (SNACS) capabilities to provide advanced sensitive mast
 technologies to improve tactical operations and reduce the risk of counter detection.

 PL830 - Periscope Production Engineering
 Production Engineering funds provide the following functions: value engineering; review and evaluation of production design data and documentation; production configuration control; maintenance engineering
 efforts designed and incorporated into the production manufacturing process, and other related engineering functions that are integral to all of the Imaging Systems and ancillary components.

 PL927 - Fleet Modernization Program Installs
 Funding is for the installation of Fleet Modernization Program Equipment.

 PL4M7 - Non-Fleet Modernization Program Installs
 Funding is for the installation of non-Fleet Modernization Program Equipment.

 The Submarine Support Equipment Program (SSEP) procures submarine Electronic Warfare (EW) systems: AN/BLQ-10, Multifunction Modular Masts (MMM) Payloads, Communications Acquisition and
 Direction-Finding (CADF) systems, Electronic Warfare Tactical Upgrade (EWTU), Field Change Kits, Submarine Tethered Expendable Buoy (STEB), the SSBN (OHIO) Collision Avoidance System, Mission
 Configurable Bay (MCB) on VA Block I/II, PATRIOT and BPS 15/16/17 RADARs. The SSEP also supports all ILS, production engineering, Cyber, sustainment, and installation requirements for EW systems. The

LI 0840 - Sub Periscope, Imaging and Supt Equip Pr...                                          UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                                                     Volume 1 - 104

Shipboard Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                      Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                 Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 Technical Insertion/Advanced Processing Build (TI/APB) SWFTS model modernizes and sustains SSNs/SSGNs through executing bi-annual software APBs and bi-annual hardware TIs. SWFTS related annual
 funding varies based on the number of modernizations funded. The Submarine EW Capability Development Document (CDD) 907-97-16 was approved on 27 September 2016 and provides all requirements
 for Submarine EW systems. Both the EWTU and the AN/BLQ-10 are required to meet the Submarine EW CDD. Procurement of the EWTU fills the gap in CADF capability in VA BLK IV to meet the EW CDD
 requirements. A gap was created when the CADF production line closed at the end of VA BLK III production and the subsequent cancellation of TI-16/18 NGA contracts, which would have replaced ICADF.

 ML003 - SSEP Special Support Equipment
 SSEP special support equipment allows for the procurement and grooming of special purpose test equipment utilized by the Type Commander Groom Teams. Exact quantities vary from year to year based on
 Fleet requirements. Beginning in FY25, procures additional special support equipment (SSE), special test equipment (STE) and spares for Submarine Surveillance Equipment Program, Pacific's (SSEP PAC's)
 increased mission support in Guam. Funding needed to certify SSNs for Western Pacific (WESTPAC) deployments and support forward deployed operations.

 ML007 - CADF
 Procures the Server Based Communications Acquisition and Direction Finding (SB-CADF) systems for installation on LOS ANGELES, SEAWOLF, VIRGINIA and COLUMBIA Class submarines.

 ML012 (P-40a) - SWFTS Supportability and Integration
 Tech Refresh provides for software and hardware updates to accommodate shifts in technology to the execution procurement years "current state-of-the-practice" hardware. Updates are necessary for signal
 and display processing hardware as software is introduced or as commercial support for the hardware is phased out. The EW Engineering Measurement Program provides analytical and testing support to
 improve design and requirements. When EW systems are being upgraded to subsequent phases, signal processing and display hardware will be upgraded to accommodate common technology consistent
 with the APB being implemented in the year of introduction. In future years, requirements include additional equipment to prevent COTS hardware from becoming unsupportable/obsolete. Funding will be
 used to support non-recurring first article test efforts associated with changing COTS environment, procuring test equipment and simulators. Supports Government Furnished Information (GFI) management
 and coordination, monitoring of contractor cost, performance and schedule, cost analysis and estimation, contract specification development and monitoring of contract deliverables. Addresses the Cyber
 requirements associated with procuring, engineering and sustaining EW systems. Also supports EW system training requirements to include curriculum development, training materials, and initial factory training
 pilot course conduct, Navy Training Plans, instructor advisory services, and hardware/software upgrades for EW trainers.

 ML012 (P-3a) - AN/BLQ-10 Technical Insertion
 Procures the AN/BLQ-10 EW system for installation on LOS ANGELES, SEAWOLF, VIRGINIA, and COLUMBIA Class submarines beginning with TI-20. AN/BLQ-10 achieves EW superiority through hardware
 and software updates to pace the threat and brings EW fully onto the SWFTS model. Tech insertion procures the hardware necessary to upgrade and back-fit EW systems. This system will be fully aligned with
 SWFTS, with the exception of SSBN (OHIO) Class, and provides capabilities required by the EW Capability Development Document (CDD).

 ML014 - Legacy Submarine Surveillance Measures
 Continues legacy Submarine Electronic Surveillance Measures (SubESM) procurement including AN/BRD-7 and AN/WLR-8 Reliability and Maintainability (R&M) obsolescence and operational Field Change
 Kits and other materials for Obsolescence Mitigation, e.g., Digital Display Unit (DDU) obsolescence upgrade, Solid State Memory, Heat Dissipation improvement, related H,M&E sail components, Analog relay
 replacement, antenna cabling replacement, related Hull, Mechanical & Electrical (H,M&E) sail components and associated Integrated Logistics Support (ILS), and technical data.

 ML017 - AN/BLQ-10 Field Change Kits
 Procures AN/BLQ-10 subsystem Product Improvement Field Change Kits including: emergent Engineering Changes, SWFTS associated Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) and technical data. Provides AN/
 BLQ-10 intermediate level repairs, changes and maintenance activities and associated materials that improve the overall Reliability, Maintainability and Availability of fielded EW systems. Procures sustainment
 efforts for AN/BLQ-10 active, deploying fleet assets. Provides funding for procurement of MMM Payload to support submarine Fleet required improved Unmanned Aerial System (UAS) communications and
 advanced EW capabilities. Procures Submarine Tethered Expendable Buoy (STEB), a 3" Launcher deployed, expendable, free-floating buoy that will communicate with the submarine through a fiber-optic
 tether. STEB provides Indications and Warning and Third Party Targeting through imaging, communications, and Radar acquisition and direction finding. Procures the Automated Range Finder (ARF) SSBN
 (OHIO) Class submarine Collision Avoidance System. This system will increase situational awareness while operating at periscope depth, improving the submarine's safety, stealth, and security. Also, updates
 the Mission Configurable Bay (MCB) on the first eight (8) Virginia Class SSNs to receive a Multifunction Modular Mast (MMM). This effort ensures all SSNs support payload required for Fleet priorities such as
 Submarine Launched Unmanned Aerial System (SLUAS) and other carry-on equipment.

 ML021 - Submarine RADAR
 Submarine RADAR funds provide AN/BPS-15/16/17 Reliability, Maintainability and Availability (RMA), Field Change Kits and material procurements for obsolescence mitigation. Provides in-service and systems
 engineering, logistics, material, configuration management, quality assurance, installation and test certification support only for the Submarine Surface Navigation (AN/BPS-15/16/17) RADAR. Provides for
 interface with the conjoined Voyage Management System (VMS) and Electronic Chart & Display Information System - Navy (ECDIS-N). Funding addresses sustainability of PATRIOT Phase B RADAR including
 repair/refurbishment, in-service engineering, and modernization.

 ML830 - EW Production Engineering
 Production Engineering funds provide the following functions: value engineering; review and evaluation of production design data and documentation; production configuration control; maintenance engineering
 efforts designed and incorporated into the production manufacturing process, and other related engineering functions that are integral to all production EW Systems and upgrades.

 ML927 - Fleet Modernization Program Installs
 Provides for the Installation of Equipment including Fleet Modernization Program Installations for shipboard systems.

 ML4M7 - Non-Fleet Modernization Program Installs
 Provides for the Installation of non-Fleet Modernization Program Equipment.

Note: Totals in this Exhibit P-40 set may not be exact or sum exactly due to rounding.

 Justification:
 The FY 2026 request for SUB PERISCOPE, IMAGING AND SUPT EQUIP PROG includes $135,863 thousand of discretionary and $141,550 thousand of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $277,413
 thousand. The mandatory funds procure, support, and install Integrated Submarine Imaging System (ISIS) and Electronic Warfare (EW) BLQ-10 systems. These systems are part of the Submarine Warfare
 Federated Tactical Systems (SWFTS), a family of interdependent systems that conduct cooperative system development, procurement, and installations. Further information for this reconciliation request is
 provided in Section 200010 (Readiness) of the Reconciliation Exhibit.

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $.315 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 Beginning in FY25, mast procurement quantities are reduced based on higher overall costs and a production backlog at the manufacturer. An annual NRE line is established to more accurately account for
 hardware costs while enabling the program to respond more adeptly to technical competition and further optimize engineering and production operations.

 As part of reconciliation request, Imaging FY26 procures TI-24 on (3) VA Class, (1) SSBN (CLB) Class, and (2) SSBN (OHIO) Class submarines and TI-26 on (1) VA Class submarine in support of Submarine
 Warfare Federated Tactical System (SWFTS). Imaging FY26 installs TI-24 on (4) VA Class and (2) SSBN (OHIO) Class submarines from the FY25 procurements. EW FY26 procures TI-24 on (3) VA Class and
 (1) SSBN (CLB) Class submarines and TI-26 on (1) VA Class submarine in support of SWFTS. EW FY26 installs TI-24 on (4) VA Class submarines from the FY25 procurements.

Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -



 Description:
 Provides for the modernization of Imaging systems and Technical Insertion kits to upgrade Integrated Submarine Imaging System (ISIS) on LOS ANGELES, SEAWOLF, VIRGINIA, OHIO SSBN/SSGN, and
 COLUMBIA Class SSBN platforms in support of Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (ISR) requirements. Unit costs for ISIS kit procurement and installation vary based on platform. VIRGINIA and
 COLUMBIA ISIS kits require a higher unit cost than the LOS ANGELES and SEAWOLF as they utilize technically advanced masts that require more sophisticated ISIS hardware and software to support them.
 Beginning with TI-16, each VIRGINIA requires a one-time critical modernization hardware update in order to field Low Profile Photonics Mast (LPPM), Type 20, and Type 24 Masts on these modernization hulls.
 This is known as Mast Compatibility (MC) and includes procurement of copper/fiber diploops and hull penetrators thus requiring a higher unit cost for VIRGINIA TI shipsets with MC. This line item aligns the
 periscope, imaging, and support equipment with the Submarine Warfare Federated Tactical System (SWFTS) PMS401 (BLI 2150) acoustic and PMS425 (BLI 5420) combat control upgrades, as well as EW AN/
 BLQ-10 equipment in ML012 and EW CADF equipment in ML007 of this budget line item. SWFTS TI shipsets must be procured and installed in like quantities (except EW SSBN (OHIO)) across all PARMs to
 maintain the SWFTS model.

ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready) :                                                                  MDAP/MAIS Code:
 (4)
    The VA TI with Mast Compatibility (MC) kit is a one-time critical modernization hardware upgrade required for every VA platform and includes funding for the procurement of the Common Electrical Hull
 Penetrator and Common Dip Loop which is required for TI-16 and beyond installations. The Common Electrical Hull Penetrator and Common Dip Loop provides the required interface for current photonics masts,
 including the LPPM and supports all future mast types. As a result, unit cost is higher than VA TI without MC.
 (5)
   FY26 part of reconciliation request. VA Technical Insertion without Mast Compatibility (MC), documented in 1.1.3 procures hardware required for TI installations on platforms which have already received the
 Common Electrical Hull Penetrator and Common Dip Loop during a prior modernization or during new construction. Unit cost is higher than 688/21 TI due to greater processing and larger footprint.

 Footnotes:
 (6)
    Funding required in FY25 to procure QTY (8) Infrared (IR) Collision Avoidance Systems for 8 SSBN (OHIO) Class submarines. This new capability will increase situational awareness for SSBNs when
 operating at periscope depth, thereby improving submarine safety, stealth, and security. All 8 systems will be procured in FY25 to realize quantity cost savings and installed over the following 3 years as platform
 schedules allow.
 (7)
        One time buy in FY25 realizes quantity cost savings in support of installations planned for FY26/27/28.

                            (The following Resource Summary rows are for informational purposes only. The corresponding budget requests are documented elsewhere.)
Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                                                                          -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -



 Description:
 VA and CLB Class advanced low profile imaging masts including Low Profile Photonics Mast (LPPM), Type 20, Type 24 and Type 2S masts. Procures initial modernization assets as well as spare masts to
 maintain overall spares ratio for photonics masts as quantity of VA and CLB Class submarines increase and special support tooling to enable the maintenance and repair of such masts at Regional Repair
 Centers. Maintaining sufficient quantities of installed and spare low profile imaging masts is essential to meet Fleet requirements for all deploying VA and CLB class submarines to be equipped with low profile
 imaging masts. Procure support equipment to allow repair of low profile masts at Regional Repair Centers which reduces Mean Time to Repair, thereby increasing Operational Availability of Low Profile Masts
 to meet Fleet requirements. Includes equipment comprising of module test set, alignment fixtures, test equipment, and repair tooling. PL025 funds Mast Capability Enhancements (MCE) and System for Non-
 Acoustics Control of Signatures (SNACS) capabilities to provide advanced sensitive mast technologies to improve tactical operations and reduce the risk of counter detection.

 Footnotes:
 (8)
        Mast Capability Enhancement (MCE) back-fits legacy photonics masts to provide advanced sensitive technologies for tactical operations.
 (9)
    In FY24, $44.555M of LPM NRE was required to address higher priority Navy requirements in the low profile imaging mast program. These include non-recurring engineering to accelerate development,
 procurement of key materials, and Submarine Industrial Base investments in the mast program. SWFTS installations will not be impacted by the FY24 procurement realignment as other imaging mast models
 were available to meet mission requirements. Beginning in FY25, mast procurement quantities are reduced and an annual NRE line is established to align with realized U/C increases from FY20-FY23 and to
 more accurately account for procurement cost of hardware vice the necessary lifecycle and non-recurring engineering costs to support the production line. These adjustments will enable the program to respond
 more adeptly to technical competition and further optimize engineering and production operations. Portion of FY26 part of reconciliation request.

1810N / 01 / 9                                                                              0840 / Sub Periscope, Imaging and Supt Equip Prog                                  4 / ML007 CADF (SWFTS)
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready) :                                                                                  MDAP/MAIS Code:

                            (The following Resource Summary rows are for informational purposes only. The corresponding budget requests are documented elsewhere.)
Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                                                                          -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -



 Description:
 Provides advanced low-band Direction Finding (DF) capability compatible with the AN/BLQ-10 EW system. The ICADF system is experiencing increasing failures and obsolescence issues shown in Operation
 Availability and Reliability, Maintainability, Availability analyses. Beginning in FY22 with TI-22, the ICADF system will be replaced by a new CADF solution integrated with the AN/BLQ-10 system. This new CADF
 solution uses server-based technology to replace obsolete and unsupportable analog systems. This upgrade is part of the drive to digitize the submarine EW systems which allows these systems to be upgraded
 more rapidly and cheaply through software solutions. This cost code incorporates the CADF equipment with the AN/BLQ-10 equipment in ML012. Therefore, CADF is a part of the SWFTS TI shipset and must
 be procured and installed in like quantities (except EW SSBN (OHIO) Class) across all PARMs to maintain the SWFTS model. EW does not do upgrades on SSBN (OHIO) Class ships. This is the only SWFTS
 subsystem that does not participate in SSBN (OHIO) TI/APB upgrades.

                                                                                                                                                                               INSERTION (SWFTS)

Less PY Advance Procurement ($ in Millions)                                                             -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -
Net Procurement (P-1) ($ in Millions)                                                              142.610                    69.744                   47.592                  0.000             0.000              0.000
Plus CY Advance Procurement ($ in Millions)                                                             -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -
Total Obligation Authority ($ in Millions)                                                         142.610                    69.744                   47.592                  0.000             0.000              0.000
                            (The following Resource Summary rows are for informational purposes only. The corresponding budget requests are documented elsewhere.)
Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                                                                          -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                    -                 -                  -



 Description:
 Procures the AN/BLQ-10 EW system for installation on LOS ANGELES, SEAWOLF, VIRGINIA, and COLUMBIA SSBN Class submarines beginning with TI-20. AN/BLQ-10 achieves EW superiority through
 hardware and software updates to pace the threat and brings EW fully onto the SWFTS model. Tech insertion procures the hardware necessary to upgrade and back-fit EW systems. This system will be fully
 aligned with SWFTS with the exception of SSBN (OHIO) Class and provides capabilities required by the EW Capability Development Document (CDD). Starting with TI-20, the AN/BLQ-10 is a more complex
 design over previous system resulting in a higher unit cost. NRE costs have been broken out from shipset costs consistent with the other SWFTS subsystems (SSN Acoustic Equipment, SSN Combat Control
 Systems, and Imaging Systems) who use the SWFTS model. This line item aligns the AN/BLQ-10 equipment with the Submarine Warfare Federated Tactical System (SWFTS) PMS401 (BLI 2150) acoustic
 and PMS425 (BLI 5420) combat control upgrades, as well as Imaging equipment in PL010 and CADF equipment in ML007 of this budget line item. SWFTS TI shipsets must be procured and installed in
 like quantities (except EW SSBN (OHIO) Class) across all PARMs to maintain the SWFTS model. EW does not do upgrades on SSBN (OHIO) Class ships. This is the only SWFTS subsystem that does not
 participate in SSBN (OHIO) TI/APB upgrades.

1810N / 01 / 9                                                                              0840 / Sub Periscope, Imaging and Supt Equip Prog                                  6 / ML017 AN/BLQ-10 FIELD CHANGE
                                                                                                                                                                               KITS
