 [P40A / CJ090 - LPD17 Class Mid-life Modernization (ExMOD)]: CJ090 LPD 17 CLASS MID-LIFE MODERNIZATION
 Conduct Mid-Life modernization on LPD 17 Class Ships in order to ensure they are ready, relevant, and capable of reaching their 40+ year Expected Service Life.

 [P40A / CJ091-ECS PIMCS/SCS GOV]: CJ091 - ECS PIMCS - SCS Gov
 Implementation of system upgrades to address the need for a common, sustainable and reliable ECS and SCS across LPD ships which will address obsolescence issues, improve circuit card reliability, reduce
 circuit card costs, increase cybersecurity posture and provide commonality in steering systems among surface ships.

 [P40A / CJ443 - Digital Rudder Angle Display System (DRADiS) (PMS443)]: CJ443 - Digital Rudder Angle Display System (DRADiS)
 Beginning in FY26 as part of the NAVSEA SEA21 reorganization, funding transferred from Cost Code CJ090 (owned by PMS321) to this newly established Cost Code (owned by PMS443). Efforts include the
 procurement and installation of DRADiS equipment, oversight support, and engineering support for the System Operational Verification Test (SOVT). An Alteration Installation Team (AIT) will perform this work as
 per a Ship Change Document (SCD). The system will provide rudder position indication to multiple displays throughout the ship, ensuring continuous Rudder Angle Indication during specific steering casualties.

 [P3A / CJ030 - HW/SW Obsolescence]: CJ030, CJ032 LPD 17 Hardware/Software HW/SW Obsolescence effort addresses critical hardware obsolescence and technology refreshment issues and supporting
 class upgrades focused on increased capability and reliability. Funding will support procurement of hardware and software changes to the systems listed:
 -Command Information Display System (CIDS) 23TV, Replace projectors with Light Emitting Diode (LED) Projectors
 -Engineering Control Systems (ECS) - Windows 10 upgrades
 -Fire Detection System Upgrade Phase 1
 -Wire-free Portable Communication System (WPCS) - Uninterrupted Power Supply (UPS) Upgrade, Low Power Amplifier Upgrade, Main Site Upgrade.
 -27TV - 28TV System Upgrade
 -Integrated Voice Communication System (IVCS) - IVCS system upgrades (local cutout switches, AFT lookout communication mod, obsolete terminal replacement, FWD/AFT Keyswitched Integrated Terminal
 Equipment (KITE)), Software version 7.2.2
 -1MC Announcing System - Digital Microphone Control Station
 -Damage Control Action Management System (DCAMS) - Upgrade to Advanced Damage Control System (ADCS)
 -Obsolete Fire Alarm Control Panel Replacement (FACP).

 [P3A - 2 / CJ040 HM&E Electrical Modifications]: CJ040 HM&E ELECTRICAL MODIFICATIONS HM&E Modernization funding is provided to correct mission, safety and reliability issues on electrical systems and
 ultimately resolve maintenance intensive system concerns. HM&E Electrical Modifications include:
 -SWAN UPS- Space Based UPS Reduction
 -Helo/Aircraft Electrical Service System (HESS/AESS).

 [P3A - 3 / CJ050 - HM&E Survivability Modifications]: CJ050 HM&E SURVIVABILITY MODIFICATIONS HM&E Modernization funding is provided to correct mission, safety and reliability issues on HM&E systems
 (impacting ship survivability) and ultimately resolve maintenance intensive system concerns. HM&E Survivability Modifications include:
 -Composite Ventilation Louvers and Screens.

 [P3A - 4 / CJ060 - HM&E Mechanical/Auxiliary Modifications]: CJ060, CJ061, CJ062, CJ063 HM&E MECHANICAL/AUXILIARY MODIFICATIONS HM&E Modernization funding is provided to correct mission,
 safety and reliability issues on mechanical and auxiliary systems and ultimately resolve maintenance intensive system concerns. HM&E Mechanical/ Auxiliary Modifications include:
 -MIDE Bulkhead Shaft Seal
 -Helo Hangar Door Control Modifications
 -2nd Pass Reverse Osmosis (RO) Unit Upgrade, RO Unit Potable Water Electrolytic Disinfectant Generator (EDG) Recirculation Capability
 -Aviation Maintenance Bridge Crane Replacement
 -Knuckleboom Crane Improvements

 -Scullery Improvements

 -Common Fuel Rail upgrades

 [P3A - 5 / CJ070 SWAN-CANES Integration]: CJ070 SWAN-CANES Integration
 The results of the Shipboard Wide Area Network (SWAN) / Consolidated Afloat Networks and Enterprise Services (CANES) study directed the replacement of the C4I capability of the LPD 17 Class SWAN with
 CANES and the conversion of the non-C4I SWAN into a new HM&E Network (mini SWAN). SWAN funding is required to field the new HM&E Network in support of the CANES installation. In addition, funding
 is required to sustain the legacy SWAN, while the LPD 17 Class ships await the CANES/HM&E Network back fit installation. SWAN serves as the backbone of the LPD 17 class and funding is necessary to
 address obsolescence, reliability issues, performance concerns, emergent Fleet requirements including enabling Cryptologic Log On (CLO), removing unsupported software, replacing aging obsolete network
 hardware (e.g., servers and core switches), and maintaining the Information Assurance posture of the Common Operating Environment (COE). This funding is vital to ensure the LPD 17 Class ships can combat
 the evolving cyber threat.

 Justification:
 The FY 2026 request for LPD Class Support Equipment includes $101,042 thousand of discretionary and $24,500 thousand of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $125,542 thousand. The mandatory funds
 support AMPHIB modernization, LPD availability procurements and LPD Midlife availabilities. Further information for this reconciliation request is provided in Section 200010 (Readiness) of the Reconciliation
 Exhibit.

 The FY 2026 increase in discretionary funding is due to a larger workload and more ship complexity scheduled in FY 2026. FY 2026 is the first year of A-2 year needed long lead time material procurements for
 LPD 18 in FY 2028 CNO availability Midlife package, accounting for $33,000K. $18.800K increase for newly appropriated CJ091 - ECS PIMCS - SCS Gov: Implementation of system upgrades to address the
 need for a common, sustainable and reliable ECS and SCS across LPD ships which will address obsolescence issues, improve circuit card reliability, reduce circuit card costs, increase cybersecurity posture and
 provide commonality in steering systems among surface ships. Based on CNO Availability schedules; CJ030, CJ061, CJ063 cost code installations have increased by $26,000K.

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $12.789 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 Footnotes:
 (1)
   CJ020 Shore Based Spares. Funds remaining are added to this line after requirements tied to CNO Availabilities are fully funded. Remaining funding amount applied to this line may vary depending on the funding amounts needed to
 support program requirements. The program has a running list of 340+ line items in support of spare needs. Priorities of procurements are determined based on funding available and timeline urgency. FY26 requirement depletion is due to
 budget reductions.
 (2)
    The FY 2026 funding decrease is due to requirement reduction is due to historical budget reductions. Conjunctive HM&E Alterations includes design integration, equipment procurement, recurring equipment engineering, equipment
 integration and Integrated Logistics Services (ILS).
 (3)
   The FY 2026 funding decrease is due to requirement reductions due to LLTM material timeline needs in support of CNO availability installation schedules. HM&E Condition System alterations include equipment procurement, software
 configuration and quality assurance, recurring equipment engineering, equipment integration and Integrated Logistics Services (ILS).
 (4)
    The FY 2026 funding increase reflects the first year of A-2 year needed long lead time material procurements for LPD18 FY28 CNO availability. FY26 to FY27 cost increase reflects LPD20's FY29 CNO availability A-2 year Shipchecks, and
 long lead time material procurement requirements in addition to ongoing LPD18 requirements. LPD 17 CLASS MID-LIFE MODERNIZATION (ExMOD). ExMOD requirements for LPD's MIDLIFE. FY 2024 was the first year of CJ090 ExMOD
 NRE requirement needed in support of first Midlife availability package for LPD 18's FY28 CNO availability. ExMOD package development requires NRE to be initiated 4 years prior of first CNO availability to definitize Class requirement and
 lethality for Extended Service Life (ESL).
 (5)
    The FY 2026 funding increase is due to engineering and first planned procurement in support of first planned implementation. Implementation of system upgrades to address the need for a common, sustainable and reliable ECS and SCS
 across LPD ships which will address obsolescence issues, improve circuit card reliability, reduce circuit card costs, increase cybersecurity posture and provide commonality in steering systems among surface ships.

 Description:
 HW/SW Obsolescence (CJ030) includes funding for procurement and installation of equipment to correct critical obsolescence issues on LPD17 Class mission systems. Resolves operational and obsolescence
 issues. Replaces current split controllers, resolves rack cooling, modifies loudspeaker cable plant, and provides commonality and system architecture and hardware.

 [CJ030-HW/SW Obsolescence] HW/SW Obsolescence effort addresses hardware obsolescence/technology refreshment issues, and class upgrades focused on increased capabilities, force protection, and
 safety. Funding will support procurement of hardware and software changes to the systems listed:
 -Command Information Display System (CIDS) 23TV - HW refresh and upgrade to Windows 10
 -Fire Detection System Upgrade Phase 1
 -Wire-free Portable Communication System (WPCS) - Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS) Upgrade, Low Power Amplifier Upgrade
 -27TV - 28TV Low Channel Pier Cable Blockage improvement
 -Integrated Voice Communication System (IVCS)- IVCS system upgrades (local cutout switches, AFT lookout communication mod, obsolete terminal replacement, FWD/AFT KITE), Software version 7.2.2.
 -1MC Announcing System - Digital Microphone Control Station 1MC improvements
 -Damage Control Action Management Software (DCAMS) - Upgrade to Advanced Damage Control System (ADCS).
 -Obsolete Fire Alarm Control Panel Replacement (FACP).

 Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within fiscal years. Each ship requires a different number of alterations to be installed, and alteration packages vary in
 scope and cost based on individual ship configurations. A quantity of "1" represents a single "kit" intended for installation in one ship, in a single Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) Availability. Ships may be
 receiving more than 1 kit in a single availability based upon the fielding plan for the systems being modernized. DSA costs will also vary by kit due to differences in scope and complexity, and first installation
 compared to follow-on installations.

 remaining install on LPD 19. Based on Start of Avail date changes, the Manufacturer Information dates have been updated to reflect reality ensuring accurate and timely delivery for new milestones, and still meet
 benchmarks. The FY 2026 funding increase is due to new Fire Alarm Control Panel (FACP) system. In FY 2026 (2) kits will be procured for installations on FY 2027 CNO Availabilities for LPD 23 (1 kit), and

 quantity remains but a portion of funding for (1) DRADIS kit has been properly aligned into cost code CJ443. Each ship requires a different number of alterations to be installed, and these alteration packages

 Description:
 CJ050 HM&E SURVIVABILITY MODIFICATIONS HM&E Modernization funding is provided to correct mission, safety and reliability issues on HM&E systems (impacting ship survivability) and ultimately resolve
 maintenance intensive system concerns. HM&E Survivability Modifications include:
 -Composite Ventilation Louvers and Screens.

 [CJ050 - HM&E Survivability Modifications] Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within a fiscal year. Each ship requires a different number of alterations to be
 installed, and these alteration packages vary in scope and cost based on individual ship configurations. A quantity of "1" represents a single "kit" intended for installation in one ship, in a single CNO Availability.
 Ships may be receiving more than 1 kit in a single availability based upon the fielding plan for the systems being modernized, and the actual requirement varies in different louver & screen sizes by increment.
 Each kit is of a different size and zone increment so they cannot be compared against other kits. DSA costs will also vary by kit due to differences in scope and complexity, and first installation compared to
 follow-on installations. CONUS ship alterations should be executed during the average installation availability timeframe of 45 days. However, OCONUS ships require 100+ days to complete installation

 Description:
 HM&E Mechanical/Auxiliary Modifications (CJ060) includes funding for procurement and installation of equipment to support upgrades of shipboard mechanical/auxiliary systems to a more sustainable, reliable
 configuration on LPD17 Class ships. Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within a fiscal year. Each ship requires a different number of alterations to be installed,
 and these alteration packages vary in scope and cost based on individual ship configurations. A quantity of "1" represents a single "kit" intended for installation in one ship, in a single CNO Availability. Ships may
 be receiving more than 1 kit in a single availability based upon the fielding plan for the systems being modernized.

 HM&E Mechanical/Auxiliary Modifications include:
 -MIDE Bulkhead Shaft Seal
 -Helo Hangar Door Control Modifications
 -2nd Pass Reverse Osmosis (RO) Unit Upgrade, RO Unit Potable Water Electrolytic Disinfectant Generator (EDG) Recirculation Capability
 -Aviation Maintenance Bridge Crane Replacement
 -Scullery Improvements
 -Branch Piping Modifications

 [CJ060 HM&E Mechanical/Auxiliary Modifications] Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within a fiscal year. Each ship requires a different number of alterations
 to be installed, and these alteration packages vary in scope and cost based on individual ship configurations. A quantity of "1" represents a single "kit" intended for installation in one ship, in a single CNO
 Availability. Ships may be receiving more than 1 kit in a single availability based upon the fielding plan for the systems being modernized. DSA costs will also vary by kit due to differences in scope and
 complexity, and first installation compared to follow-on installations. The program production lead times have increased from 12 months to 15 months based on actual procurement buys to date.

 Description:
 CJ070 SWAN-CANES Integration
 The results of the Shipboard Wide Area Network (SWAN) / Consolidated Afloat Networks and Enterprise Services (CANES) study directed the replacement of the C4I capability of the LPD 17 Class SWAN with
 CANES and the conversion of the non-C4I SWAN into a new HM&E Network (mini SWAN). SWAN funding is required to field the new HM&E Network in support of the CANES installation. In addition, funding
 is required to sustain the legacy SWAN, while the LPD 17 Class ships await the CANES/HM&E Network back fit installation. SWAN serves as the backbone of the LPD 17 class and funding is necessary to
 address obsolescence, reliability issues, performance concerns, emergent Fleet requirements including enabling Cryptologic Log On (CLO), removing unsupported software, replacing aging obsolete network
 hardware (e.g., servers and core switches), and maintaining the Information Assurance posture of the Common Operating Environment (COE). This funding is vital to ensure the LPD 17 Class ships can combat
 the evolving cyber threat.

 Description:
 NRE includes the following efforts:

 [CJ032-WPCS Main Site Upgrades] WIRELESS PORTABLE COMMUNICATIONS SYSTEM (WPCS) Hardware/Software (CJ032): HW/SW Obsolescence effort addresses critical hardware obsolescence and
 technology refreshment issues and supporting class upgrades focused on increased capability and reliability. Funding will support procurement of hardware and software changes to the WPCS Main Site.

 Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within a fiscal year. Each ship requires a different number of alterations to be installed, and these alteration packages vary
 in scope and cost based on individual ship configurations. A quantity of "1" represents a single "kit" intended for installation in one ship, in a single Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) Availability. Ships may be
 receiving more than 1 kit in a single availability based upon the fielding plan for the systems being modernized. Requirements are subject to changes in the CNO ship availability schedule. DSA costs will also
 vary by kit due to differences in scope and complexity, and first installation compared to follow-on installations.

Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                                                                          -                         -                         -                     -                 -                   -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                     -                 -                   -



 Description:
 [CJ061-Titanium Valves Replacement Phase 3] Scheduling differences between Phases 1, 2, & 3 are all based on size of valves (8 inches, 10 inches, & 12 inches) and location of valves in the hull, for better
 installation planning.
 Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within a fiscal year. Each ship requires a different number of alterations to be installed, and these alteration packages vary
 in scope and cost based on individual ship configurations. A quantity of "1" represents a single "kit" intended for installation in one ship, in a single Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) Availability. Ships may be
 receiving more than 1 kit in a single availability based upon the fielding plan for the systems being modernized. DSA costs will also vary by kit due to differences in scope and complexity, and first installation
 compared to follow-on installations. All Phase III procurements have been moved to A-2 years prior to installation, vice A-1 year prior to installation, due to actual lead times encountered on previously procured
 ship sets.

 Description:
 [CJ062-Knuckleboom Crane Improvement] KNUCKLEBOOM CRANE IMPROVEMENTS Modernization funding is provided to correct mission, safety and reliability issues on mechanical and auxiliary systems
 and ultimately resolve maintenance intensive system concerns. Funding will support boat and cargo crane safety upgrades.

 A quantity of (1) FY21 installations originally budgeted (FY20 request) for LPD 19 has been deferred to future CNO Availability schedule.
 Shipsets of equipment vary in quantities and types of components each fiscal year and within a fiscal year. Each ship requires a different number of alterations to be installed, and these alteration packages vary
 in scope and cost based on individual ship configurations. A quantity of "1" represents a single "kit" intended for installation in one ship, in a single Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) Availability. Ships may be
 receiving more than 1 kit in a single availability based upon the fielding plan for the systems being modernized. DSA costs will also vary by kit due to differences in scope and complexity, and first installation
 compared to follow-on installations.

 Description:

 Description:
 DDG 1000, a multi-mission surface combatant, will serve as a versatile asset in the context of future Naval Strategy. Armed with an array of weapons, DDG 1000 will provide the Joint Force Commander with
 precision strike and volume fires. Designed with sustainable payload, multi-spectral stealth and optimal manning, DDG 1000 will take the fight to the enemy with unprecedented striking power.

 The program is continuing to integrate the Conventional Prompt Strike (CPS) weapons system onto the Zumwalt class. CPS integration is a top CNO priority that enhances the Navy's lethality by providing a
 critical hypersonic attack capability to prevail in Great Power competition. This naval surface hypersonic strike capability will allow the Zumwalt class to conduct independent forward deployed operations and
 prosecute deep-inland, time-critical, soft and medium-hardened targets in a contested environment. Additionally, when combined with the low observable characteristics of the Zumwalt platform, CPS enhances a
 uniquely lethal and survivable naval surface platform. The Zumwalt Class will enhance U.S. conventional power projection by providing an independent forward deployed strike platform, with longer range, shorter
 time of flight, and higher survivability against enemy defenses compared to current capabilities.

 The Navy will integrate CPS into USS Zumwalt (DDG 1000) and USS Michael Monsoor (DDG 1001) during Building Yard Modernization Periods (BYMP) instead of traditional Docking Selected Restricted
 Availabilities (DSRA). This shipyard period type maximizes learning and efficiency in order to speed the capability to the fleet. The program will integrate CPS into Lyndon B. Johnson (DDG 1002) during new
 construction, prior to the ship's arrival in homeport and prior to installation of CPS on DDG 1001. Full CPS operational capability on Zumwalt Class is achieved once CPS is installed in all three ships and they are
 turned over for Fleet operations.

 DDG 1000 and DDG 1001 are commissioned ships and the CPS installations for these ships is budgeted with Other Procurement, Navy (OPN) funding. DDG 1002 CPS installation is budgeted in Shipbuilding &
 Conversion, Navy (SCN) funding. Advanced Payload Modules (APM) are interchangeable end items and are budgeted in OPN.

 The program provides for DDG 1000 combat system hardware/equipment suite technology refresh to support obsolescence management and address reliability, maintainability & availability (RM&A) issues
 following fielding. Combat System hardware will be over a decade obsolete at the time of delivery. Active management of commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) obsolescence via hardware/equipment technology
 refresh will be required to ensure Zumwalt class ships reach their design service life. Additionally, the program will sustain and upgrade in-service Zumwalt ships in order to achieve their Expected Service Life
 (ESL). Upgrades will be mission capable in countering current and projected threats, improve operational capabilities, and comply with requirements.

 [P40A / DDG 1000 Class Product Improvement]: Funding provides engineering changes to the Combat System necessary to address component obsolescence, recurring cybersecurity requirements and a
 20-module SM-6 capability on DDG 1000, during her BYMP. This effort develops or updates drawings and technical documentation, develops work specifications and technical documentation to integrate


LI 0947 - DDG 1000 Class Support Equipment                                                              UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                                                     Volume 1 - 381

Exhibit P-40, Budget Line Item Justification: PB 2026 Navy                                                                                                            Date: June 2025
Appropriation / Budget Activity / Budget Sub Activity:                         P-1 Line Item Number / Title:
1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 01: Ships Support Equipment / BSA 9: Other 0947 / DDG 1000 Class Support Equipment
Shipboard Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                        Program Elements for Code B Items: 0204202N                               Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: 197
 planned changes into the baseline design. Material will be procured, kitted, delivered and installed on the ship. Planning, prioritization and installation of ship alterations (ShipAlts) will be done during maintenance
 availability periods within the Fleet Modernization Program. Logistics product updates are provided to ensure latest ship's crew proficiency and understanding. Funding also supports software licenses and
 associated cyber security hardware, necessary to prevent vulnerabilities. Procures shipboard assets as well as Zumwalt Training Facility updates, to maintain similar configuration.

 [P40A / Critical Cybersecurity Upgrades and Improvements]: The TSCE integrates all the ship's sensors and weapons into a cohesive Combat System (CS). Funding is required for engineering and hardware
 procurement to support Windows 10 Upgrade, Linux Kernel Integrity Validation tool, and Secure Data Distribution Services. Windows 10 upgrade is necessary to prevent cyber security vulnerabilities and
 is mandated by OPNAV requirements. Linux Kernel Integrity Validation provides methods for detecting malicious activity (including "Zero-day" attacks) in order to keep pace with ongoing threats. Secure
 Data Distribution Services provides detection and countermeasure capabilities to ensure data confidentiality and integrity. Funding supports procurement of shipboard assets for deployment capability and
 configuration alignment with the ZUMWALT Training Facility.

 [P40A / TSCE Diminishing Manufacturing Sources & Material Shortages (DMSMS)]: FY24 funding supports DDG 1002's SPY-3 refurbishment, including procurement and replacement of failed or obsolescent
 materiel. DDG 1002's SPY-3 array was previously utilized on self-defense test ship, requiring refurbishment prior to install on DDG 1002. FY25 OPN funding is required to procure ZUMWALT Class ships network
 and computing equipment to address obsolescence and DMS concerns to sustain the ZUMWALT Class TSCE and minimize total operation costs. Funding is required to maintain ship readiness; without funding,
 obsolescence in ZUMWALT Class network and computing environments will impact the ship's ability to get underway and decrease capacity for sparing. Non-recurring engineering (NRE) is based on SD-22
 solution costs adjusted for inflation for current and predicted obsolescence. Funding for TSCE DMSMS funds projected and unplanned DMSMS Cases, both life of type buy and redesign of major components
 within TSCE. Current DMSMS percentage of the TSCE configuration is 85-95% obsolete. DMSMS cases will increase in severity as components within the system become end of life. The current configuration
 requires support through FY27 until TSCE modernization commences on the final ship.

 [P40A / TSCE Modernization Engineering]: Combat system computing infrastructure (data centers, network infrastructure & communications devices) is more than a decade old and requires extensive
 modernization and technology refresh to sustain operations, mitigate obsolescence, provides a hardware baseline and software development processes that align to Integrated Combat System efforts. Funding
 provides non-recurring engineering labor necessary for integration, testing, and deployment of new computing hardware and peripherals. FY23 funded software architecture development to adapt the combat
 system software to the upgraded hardware configuration. The increase in FY24 funds detailed design of hardware configuration, migration of the software baseline into a containerized architecture, and lab
 integration of modified software with new hardware, as well as software and hardware engineering to address any issues discovered during testing. Thorough lab integration is required prior to installation of
 shipboard equipment starting in FY25. The Zumwalt-class Operational Availability (Ao), as specified in the Operational Requirements Document (ORD), is the driving requirement for TSCE modernization.
 Additionally, the program is procuring life-type buys to support the ships through the start of DDG 1001 availability in FY26.

 [P40A / TSCE Moderization Lab Equipment]: Funding provides network and data center equipment at both the OEM lab and the Zumwalt Training Facility to represent the tactical configuration for the purposes
 of integration, testing, support, and training. Network equipment encompasses routers, switches, wireless access points, and a variety of audio/visual peripherals for communication and security. Data center
 equipment includes core processing, data storage, and encryption devices. This ship-representative equipment is also necessary for troubleshooting any issues that are discovered on the ships, in order to
 provide continuous fleet support.

 [P40A / TSCE Modernization Procurement]: Follow-on TSCE modernization procurement for DDG 1001 occurred in FY25; the Navy is currently assessing the plan for DDG 1000 as shipset funding is still
 required. TSCE Modernization hardware procurements are phased based on the 23 month procurement lead time.

 [P40A / HM&E Land Based Test Environment (LBTE)]: Procure material and equipment for LBTE will lead to greater understanding and competency for DDG 1000 controls systems and will lead to less
 dependency on the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) to provide life-cycle support for hardware, software and cyber security.

 [P40A / Data Center Lab (Combat System Maintenance and Hardware)]: Funding is to populate the contractor's software development lab with tactical element hardware and to establish a government integration
 lab. Currently the contractor's lab is utilizing DDG 1002 assets (SGS/AC, CDLMS/NGC2P, IFF, etc.), which will be removed to support DDG 1002 combat system installation during the combat availability. To
 maintain the contractor lab capability, additional hardware will need to be procured.

Shipboard Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                        Program Elements for Code B Items: 0204202N                               Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: 197
 [P40A / Electro-Optical / Infrared (EO/IR) Sensor]: DDG 1000 is a low radar cross section (RCS) vessel that utilizes EO/IR tactically. In conjunction with NAVSEA 05 and the Technical Warrant Holder (TWH),
 conducted a study of available EO/IR sensors and have selected the compliant sensor available to meet both the RCS and EO/IR functional requirements of DDG 1000.

 [P40A / C4I (Business) Network Sustainment]: TSCE includes a C4I (business) network, which currently resides on an unsupportable Windows 7 operating system with no sustainment plan in place. Funding
 will upgrade the current C4I (business) network from Windows 7 to Windows 10, procure licenses, and replace laptops. Critical Cyber security Upgrades and Improvements funded the upgrade of the TSCE to
 Windows 10. This did not fund upgrades to any of the applications that utilize the TSCE network such as COMPOSE 4.0.1, GCCS-M, NGC2P, etc. Since there is no program of record support for COMPOSE
 4.0.1 as installed on DDG 1000, this funds an upgrade to COMPOSE 4.0.1 to Windows 10, and provides a sustainment path for this system.

 [P40A / Radar Absorbing Material (RAM) and Composite Sustainment]: Recurring OPN is required to procure, install and repair RAM on board the DDG 1000 Class.

 [P40A / MK 57 CEU Technical Development Package]: Funding required for engineering development efforts to prepare and approve the DDG 1000 class MK57 universal canister electrical unit (UCEU) technical
 data package (TDP). The TDP is necessary to issue a build-to-print request for proposal to industry, for development and test the UCEU hardware. Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren will be the primary
 activity developing the TDP. The procurement and testing of the UCEU will be funded from OPN LI 5231 Ship Missile Support Equipment.

 [P40A / Common Electric Module Shock Kits]: Common Electric Module Shock Kits (CEMs) - Shock kits procured in FY21 to support CEM upgrade from obsolete version 2.1 to 4.0. These shock kits were
 necessary to ruggedize the new CEM 4.0 modules within the enclosure. CEM 4.0 upgrade has since been implemented across the class, and now gives the crew ability to sustain the Common Display System,
 operate and maintain the TSCE system, and continue activation of the ZUMWALT combat system.

 [P40A / DDG 1000 SM-6 Integration]: Integration of SM-6 capability. Funding required for TSCE Integration (OPN), testing, and Live Fire Testing (RDT&E). Funding will procure the Vertical Launch System
 Global Positioning System Integration (VGI) components for the necessary ephemeris data.

 [P40A / DDG 1000 NTCDL Integration]: Upgrade Tactical Common Data Link (TCDL) to Network Tactical Common Data Link (NTCDL) by removing TCDL from the ship's 07-level and replacing with deckhouse
 mounted NTCDL. TCDL is a Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) antenna which Naval Information Warfare Systems Command supports whereas NTCDL is a full program of record. Will increase capacity for
 Common Data Link (CDL) links, as well as freeing up 07-level space and improving RCS to allow for installation of other antennas. Improves signature and increases capacity of CDL links.

 [P40A / DDG 1000 SPECTRAL Integration]: Spectral is the next generation Information Warfare weapons system enhancing signals exploitation capabilities. Spectral is scalable, mission-configurable, and
 modular to rapidly respond to new threats or capabilities. A stronger, faster, integrated capability enables data sharing with other systems to support Electromagnetic Maneuver Warfare (EMW)/Integrated Fires
 (IF). Spectral detects, classifies, and tracks Signals of Interest (SOI) beyond current stated requirements to improve automation, operability, and intuitiveness in the Tasking, Collection, Processing, Exploitation,
 and Dissemination (TCPED) process. Agile software methodology ensures flexibility in meeting emergent requirements and addressing Fleet user priorities

 [P3A / Conventional Prompt Strike]: Perform shipbuilder procurements and modifications for installation of CPS on DDG 1000 Class. To include planning, design, removal forward and aft Advanced Gun
 Weapons System (MT61 and MT62 respectively), HM&E material, combat system integration, and procurement/installation of advanced payload modules.

 Justification:
 FY25 to FY26 decrease is due to no TSCE Mod procurements in FY26, and CPS Advanced Payload Module (APM) procurement quantity decrease (3 in FY25, 2 in FY26).

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $.412 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 The program is proceeding with Conventional Prompt Strike (CPS) installation on DDG 1000 and DDG 1001 during Build Yard Modernization Periods (BYMPs), which will replace the previously scheduled
 Docking Selected Restricted Availabilities (DSRAs). The DDG 1000 BYMP began execution in FY24 and the DDG 1001 BYMP will begin execution in FY26. To support the BYMPs, the program established a
 sole-source contract with Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) for production planning and execution. DDG 1002 procurement and installation costs for CPS are included in SCN for that hull.

 TSCE Critical Cybersecurity Upgrades and Improvements funding is required for engineering and hardware procurement to support Windows 10 Upgrade, Linux Kernel Integrity Validation tool, and Secure Data
 Distribution Services. Validation provides methods for detecting malicious activity (including "Zero-day" attacks) in order to keep pace with ongoing threats. Secure Data Distribution Services provides detection
 and countermeasure capabilities to ensure data confidentiality and integrity.

 TSCE DMSMS Existing DDG 1000 and follow hardware is more than a decade obsolete. Efforts includes NRE required to identify and qualify applicable obsolete parts, prepare drawing updates (Lowest
 Replaceable Unit (LRU) & cabinet level), prepare Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) updates (LRU & cabinet level) and procure replacement parts. DMSMS procurements are necessary until DDG 1001
 commences TSCE Modernization in FY27 (hardware procurement in FY25).

 TSCE Modernization Engineering funding in FY26 for continued lab integration prior to installing shipboard equipment on the first TSCE Modernization hull (DDG 1002) in FY26.

 (†)
       indicates the presence of a P-5a


 Footnotes:
 (1)
    FY26 DMSMS funding will be used to execute life of type buys (LTBs) and develop ECP/redesign work-arounds to keep the system's legacy configuration running on the remaining ships while they await TSCE Modernization. DMSMS
 cases are predicted to increase in severity each year as components within the system continue to approach end of life. Legacy configuration HW will require support through timeframe until TSCE modernization commences on the final ship.
 The $2.5M increase in FY26 will cover a one-time LTB for LCEM 4.0 modules. These LCEM 4.0 modules were recently discovered to have an estimated end-of-life date within the next couple years. Since TSCE Mod will not be upgrading
 these LCEMs, an LTB will need to be executed to keep the 4.0 LCEMs supported until a new CEM/CDS design is available in about 10 years.
 (2)
       TSCE modernization procurement for DDG 1001 occurred in FY25; the Navy is currently assessing the plan for DDG 1000. TSCE Modernization hardware procurements are phased based on the 23 month procurement lead time.

 Footnotes:
 (3)
   Funding provides network and data center equipment at both the OEM lab and the Zumwalt Training Facility to represent the tactical configuration for the purposes of integration, testing, support, and training. Network equipment
 encompasses routers, switches, wireless access points, and a variety of audio/visual peripherals for communication and security. Data center equipment includes core processing, data storage, and encryption devices. FY22 includes 1
 Network lab set, 1 Data Center lab set. FY23 includes 1 spares for lab network, 1 spares for data center, 1 data center for training facility. FY24 includes 1 Network lab set, 1 Data Center lab set.

Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                                                                          -                         -                         -                     -                 -                  -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                     -                 -                  -



 Description:
 FY26 Conventional Prompt Strike (CPS) Shipbuilding, Support, and Installation funding will support work specs for Advanced Gun System (AGS) removal, CPS HM&E modifications, and identifying CPS space
 requirements. The planning yard will address arrangements, primary structure, and existing distributive system impacts and reconstruction.

 FY26 Advanced Payload Module (APM) funding will procure 2 end item units to be installed on DDG 1000 Class ships during the BYMP or windows of opportunity following the BYMP. Advanced Payload
 Modules (APM) are interchangeable end items and are budgeted in OPN for all DDG 1000 Class hulls.

 FY25 to FY26 decrease is due to long lead material procurements in support of the DDG 1001 BYMP being completed in FY25, as well as a decrease in CPS Advanced Payload Module (APM) procurement
 quantities (3 in FY25 & 2 in FY26).

 [CPS - Advanced Payload Modules] FY26 OPN funds required to procure 2 Advanced Payload Modules (APMs) from Lockheed Martin. The production lead time for APMs is 30 months. Delivery schedule for
 APMs will be in 3 month intervals based on ability to achieve desired industrial capacity. The installation period required for APMs can occur during windows of opportunity for the designated ship.

 Unit Cost Increase on FY25 and FY26 APMs due to inflation and commodity price volatility.

 [AGS Removal, CPS HM&E Modifications, Shore Support Services] Will support work specs for Advanced Gun System (AGS) removal and identifying CPS space requirements. The planning yard shall address
 arrangements, primary structure, and existing distributive system impacts and reconstruction.
 In addition, this funding will provide waterfront support and installation teams across industry and government.
 Contract Award for DDG 1000 Build Yard Modernization Period (BYMP) occurred on December 27, 2023. The industry yard for the BYMP is Huntington Ingalls Industries - Ingalls Shipbuilding located in
 Pascagoula, MS.
 FY23/24 Funding - DDG 1000
 FY25/26 funding - DDG 1001

 Description:
 Funding in this P-1 line provides for the procurement of tactical Hull, Mechanical and Electrical (HM&E) equipment that will be installed aboard ships and in the facilities at the TRIDENT Refit Facility (TRF), Navy
 Intermediate Maintenance Facility (IMF) and TRIDENT Training Facility (TTF). TRF and Navy IMF are dedicated shore support facilities providing a full range of industrial support. TTF provides realistic training
 experience in operating and maintaining shipboard equipment for the OHIO Class (SSBN/SSGN) Submarines crews. SSBN/SSGN Submarines operate with two separate crews and TTF is critical in maintaining
 proficiency and certification for continued at-sea operations when not underway.

 The magnitude of this funding line is driven by the operational need for modernization and safety alterations. Funding in FY26 supports procurement and installation of critical Ohio Class HM&E Modernization
 including the additional mitigation efforts of a Missile Compartment Thermal Event (MCTE) to include Passive Fire Protection, and procurement of Electrical Hull Penetrators (EHPs). HM&E Modernization funding
 enables PMS396 to respond to fleet requests for HM&E modernization for safety and reliability alterations. Allows for continued operation and increased supportability of systems, such as Obsolete Equipment
 Replacement for Ship Control Subsystem upgrades, Life Support Systems upgrades, Torpedo Tube Fiber Optic Interface Upgrade, SSBN TRIDENT Planned Equipment Replacement Program (TRIPER)
 Obsolescence, SSBN Emergency Diesel Generator (EDG), EDG Rotatable Pool program, and Emergency Diesel Generator Control Panel (DGCP).

 Funding in this P-1 line also provides funding for Non-Propulsion Electronics Equipment (NPES) that will be installed aboard TRIDENT Class submarines as part of the Obsolete Equipment Replacement (OER)
 Program. Beginning in FY25, all requirements are re-aligned under Budget Line Item 0950 due to the zero-sum transfer of funds from Budget Line Item 2676 into 0950. OER Program is the replacement of
 existing hardware/software that, though functional, has become operationally obsolete, is no longer in production or supportable with spare parts, or has a high failure rate making them no longer cost effective
 to maintain. OER hardware/software changes are expected to provide significant cost savings via reduced maintenance costs and use Commercial-Off-The-Shelf (COTS) technology wherever possible as long
 as all technical requirements are met. This funding line includes performance of the required fully integrated system level testing and certification of changes to the TRIDENT Combat systems prior to installation
 of the changes on the ship. Integrated testing and certification provide assurance that when the changes are installed in the ship, the TRIDENT Combat system will operate as designed, allowing the ships to
 maintain their operational schedules and mission capabilities.

 [P40A / HH009 EQUIPMENT HM&E & SWS/SS ALTERATION]: This provides for the replacement of obsolete equipment to maintain or increase mission capabilities, replacement/modification of equipment
 identified as unreliable to reduce fleet maintenance, or correct safety items on board OHIO Class Submarines and at dedicated Shore Support Facilities. This line provides equipment procurement and
 installation, technical planning, training, and associated resources.

 [P40A / SCS SHIPALT DEV AND SUSTAINMENT]: Ship Control System (SCS) resolves interoperability issues and provides periodic system upgrades to mitigate obsolescence.


LI 0950 - Strategic Platform Support Equip                                                              UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                                                     Volume 1 - 397

Appropriation / Budget Activity / Budget Sub Activity:                         P-1 Line Item Number / Title:
1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 01: Ships Support Equipment / BSA 9: Other 0950 / Strategic Platform Support Equip
Shipboard Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                          Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                     Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A

 [P40A / SWSS (VAMP MOD 3)]: SSBN Strategic Weapon Support Systems (SWSS) program maintains the SWSS surveillance, modernization and obsolescence management program. This line monitors and
 predicts SWSS obsolescence issues and plans for program modernization to ensure OHIO class submarines continue to meet mission requirements until replaced by the COLUMBIA Class. Items anticipated
 may include; Harvesting Critical Components, Reverse Engineering, Form-Fit-Function replacements, providing lifetime buys, providing component-level SHIPALTs (reactive). This includes the development of
 SWSS Ventilation and Alarm Monitoring Panel(VAMP) MOD 3 solution, which modernizes the equipment to ensure the SWSS program continues to effectively function.

 [P40A / SWSS Sustainability]: Complete Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE), Ship Alteration Development and qualification for replacement of major Strategic Weapons Support Systems (SWSS) components and
 systems. Modernizes OHIO Class submarines SWSS with COLUMBIA technology. The SWSS System is a 1970's analog system that requires modernization to support SSBN. Current OHIO SWSS components
 will not support end of service life (EOSL). Identification of SWSS obsolescence issues continues to increase as the equipment ages with no advanced warning. The inability to obtain parts are impacting Ao
 of the SWSS after workarounds have been exhausted. Failure to address obsolescence results in loss of system capability, reduction in Ao, and impacts the ability of the SSBN platform to achieve the 42-year
 service life. Replacing obsolete SWSS OHIO class components with qualified COLUMBIA class technology minimizes development and qualification effort, mitigates occurrence of unexpected obsolescence
 issues, and establishes common equipment baseline for remainder of fleet service life.

 [P40A / Ohio Class HM&E Modernization]: OHIO Class Hull, Mechanical and Electrical (HM&E) Modernization funds initial cost estimates and planning as well as resolutions to safety, obsolescence, and
 interoperability issues for lower cost efforts that will not be tracked independently. Previous budget cycles prioritized funding away from this line to other HM&E modernization efforts resulting in a growing
 backlog. TRIDENT Hull, Mechanical and Electrical systems require modernization for systems safety to address key sustainment issues. These efforts are considered must-fund to address safety, reliability, and
 Ao risks. Provides funding to support design and development of safety and reliability efforts to address sustainment or obsolescence issues.

 [P40A / Missile Compartment Thermal Event (MCTE)]: Missile Compartment Thermal Event (MCTE), commences in FY24, providing for non-recurring engineering (NRE) and alternation (ALT) development,
 installation material, and installation of equipment (lockers, detectors, cabling) and structural/system modification (bulkheads, piping, valves). Alteration mitigates a high-risk vulnerability identified on strategic
 assets by providing defense-in-depth (preventing a fire, detecting a fire, containing a fire, suppressing a fire, extinguishing a fire, and slowing the heat transfer to the Missile Tubes).

 Beginning in FY26, this line also funds procurement and installation of Passive Fire Protection (PFP) materials (Promat microporous insulation wrap and NoFire A18-NV coating intumescent paint) on all SSBN
 missile tubes. Passive Fire Protection provides ample mitigation to reduce the risk associated with a sustained thermal event in the Missile Compartment to an acceptable level. Job hazard analysis estimates an
 unprotected tube will reach the critical temperature in 27-28 minutes while a fully protected tube will take ~300 minutes. The PFP solution was briefed to and accepted by all stakeholders at the Three Star Missile
 Compartment Thermal Event brief on 10 October 2023. PFP minimizes the potential impact caused by a Missile Compartment thermal event to the strategic deterrence mission.

 [P40A / Electric Hull Penetrators]: Beginning in FY26, this line funds the procurement of Electrical Hull Penetrators (EHPs) at the rate necessary to support current fleet demands based on historical failure rate
 of 1 EHP/Hull/QTR. There is currently zero-stock NSS EHPs for SSBNs. Lack of stock is creating delays in the installation/repairs of equipment, deferred maintenance and preventing continued effort of Depth
 Mode Noise (DMN) investigation, degrading Strategic Weapons Systems (SWS) Navigation Subsystem accuracy. This effort replenishes the stock. The failure of these components themselves is a great risk to
 the safety of the ship given the opportunity for severe water damage. Addresses the need to support the DoD's #1 mission, Nuclear Strategic Deterrence.

 [P40A / Heat and Smoke Detection Annunciator Network (HASDAN)]: HASDAN modernization resolves a high-risk vulnerability noted for OHIO Class strategic weapons system spaces to detect and respond to
 a major fire in a low manning condition (i.e. refit or overhaul) situation. An installed fire detection system alleviates this vulnerability by allowing for rapid recognition and response to a fire casualty. Investment in
 this modernizations will mitigate production/manning costs to establish/disestablish temporary systems during repair/modernization periods.

 [P40A / Life Support System (LPE) Procurement]: The Submarine Life Support System provides necessary design/development, procurement, and installation efforts to address required modernization changes
 to the Submarine Life Support Systems. Oxygen Generators are necessary for life support and to maintain submarine stealth while on mission. This funding currently funds replacement of the Electrolytic Oxygen
 Generator (EOG) with a Low Pressure Electroyzer (LPE) on both SSBN and SSGN submarines. The LPE produces oxygen at low pressure, eliminating the need for high pressure oxygen storage. There will
 be two LPEs on each SSBN/SSGN Class submarine. EOG's must be replaced by LPE's on OHIO SSBNs to ensure they can sustain current operational availability requirements through their 42 yr service life.
 Therefore, procurements are driven by the OHIO Class maintenance period schedule. The replacement of the EOG with the LPE must be done during a major maintenance availability due to the time to complete
 the installation. Future efforts will include design and development efforts for the Central Atmosphere Monitoring System (CAMS) sustainment for SSGN/BN classes.

 [P40A / Combat Control System (CCS) Revision Eng/Cert Test]: This program develops upgrades to legacy systems to maintain required interface connectivity to other Program Acquisition Resource Manager
 (PARM) managed subsystems in response to PARMs modernizations, or to address obsolescence, or where mandated requirement changes (example Cyber Security) drive changes to hardware or software.

 [P40A / SSBN Legacy CCS Modernization]: This program is critical to maintaining OHIO Class SSBN operational to the end of service life and delivery of COLUMBIA Class SSBN replacements. The program
 resolves Data Processing Subsystem (DPS) obsolescence issues and conjunctively supports the upgrade of the SSBN legacy CCS to the Submarine Warfare Federated Tactical Systems (SWFTS) architecture
 common with the SSN/SSGN classes. Collectively, the DPS and SWFTS upgrades modify Fire Control, SONAR, Voyage Management System (VMS), RADAR, Tactical Navigation, and Periscopes, and
 collaterally impact Ship Control, Monitoring Subsystem, and interfaces to ship sensors such as speed, depth, and Extremely High Frequency Satellite Communication navigation data. The DPS upgrade includes
 replacing all UYK-43 computers, DPS workstation, TRIDENT Signal Data Converters (TSDC), and the UGC-143 with a modified Electronic Control Display Unit (ECDU). The ECDU is common equipment on
 VA Class and planned for the COLUMBIA Class. The ECDU requires hardware and software development to interface to the Strategic Weapon System (SWS) Navigation and maintain critical Ship to SWS
 interface requirements. The ECDU and all the associated CCS architecture modifications are required to be installed conjunctively with SWFTS. Continued operation of the SSBN strategic mission will require the
 implementation of the ECDU upgrade along with the SWFTS upgrade.

 [P40A / TT Fiber Optic Interface Upgrade]: New weapons systems such as the MK 48 Mod 8 / Mod 9 torpedo use a fiber optic guidance wire interface rather than the legacy copper wire system. This requires
 installation of a new torpedo tube penetrator. Funding is required to design, procure, and install new penetrators on all in service SSBNs.

 [P40A / SSBN TRIPER Obsolescence]: TRIDENT Planned Equipment Replacement (TRIPER) program for Hull, Mechanical and Electrical systems require modernization to address key sustainment issues.
 HM&E systems on these boats have been maintained utilizing modernization at the lowest repairable unit to address obsolescence issues. This funding will resolve specific issues related to HM&E systems no
 longer supportable.

 [P40A / SSBN Emergency Diesel Generator (EDG) Sustainment]: TYCOM, NAVSEA 08, NAVSEA 05, SSBN PEO priority tasking for modernization efforts related to risk reduction for Emergency Diesel
 Generators. Efforts focus on reducing hard shutdowns, improving lubrication from cold start, and resolving obsolescence issues. Funding provides for NRE and ALT development of multiple EDG upgrades,
 procurement of installation material, and onboard installation of equipment to include structural/system modification to support installation.

 [P40A / EDG Rotable Pool Program]: The Emergency Diesel Generator (EDG) Rotatable Pool is critical to reducing risk to availabilities by ensuring long-lead time parts are kitted and available for execution.
 Funding will also go towards resolving TRIPER components that are coming to the end of economic reparability that are no longer available for procurement and require engineering efforts to resolve
 obsolescence.

 [P40A / SSBN EDG Modernization - DGCP (Control Panel)]: Emergency Diesel Generator Control Panel (DGCP) replaces an obsolete system. This modernized, digital DGCP, will replace the legacy DGCP
 and address numerous obsolescence issues within the cabinet itself. This effort enables improvement such as Reverse Power Monitoring and logging, Main Bearing Temperature logging and alarms, and Firing
 Temperature and Pressure alarms and logging, and vibration monitoring, logging, and analysis. Ao improvements are expected due to a combination of having parts available instead of rolling cannibalizations
 and the early identification of negative trends within both the Diesel and Generator components. EDG alterations will modernize the diesel and generator monitoring systems, addressing the concerns of
 COMSUBFOR, NAVSEA 08, and NAVSEA 05.

 [P40A / Oversight - AIT Installation Support (SIB)]: Submarine Industrial Base (SIB) funding for required SSBN oversight and availability execution associated with Alternation Installation Team (AIT) installations.

 [P40A / HH012 EQUIPMENT HM&E TRIDENT ENGINGEERING AVAILABILITY]: TRIDENT Engineered Availability (EA) support funding is required to provide system engineering, technical, logistic and
 configuration management support for all accomplished modernization during the SSBN 726 Class Submarine Engineered Availabilities (EAs). This program also provides funding for Quality Assurance (QA)
 oversight and certification by Supervisor Of Shipbuilding, Groton (SOS Groton) of SHIPALTS performed by Electric Boat Corporation (EB), provides funding to the Planning Yard for Configuration Record change
 and Liaison Action Request processing, and Selected Record Drawings mark ups for SHIPALT-related work, accomplishment of availability work package requirements assigned to the Planning Yard such as
 Design Review hanger inspections, Integrated Logistic Support product updates, and Configuration Overhaul Planning Product development and distribution for each availability. The program also provides

 funding for NUWC Newport system-level final end-to-end certification test development and onboard test conduct and analysis to support end of CNO availability certification of entire Command and Control

 Justification:
 Funding in FY26 supports procurements of Electrical Hull Penetrators (EHPs), a re-stock effort to support failure rates and procures materials for the Missile Compartment Thermal Event (MCTE) Passive Fire
 Protection program. Additionally, increased funding in FY26 supports procurement and installation of critical Ohio Class HM&E Modernization. HM&E Modernization funding enables PMS396 to respond to fleet
 requests for HM&E modernization for safety and reliability alterations. This allows for continued operation and increased supportability of systems, such as Obsolete Equipment Replacement for Ship Control
 Subsystem upgrades, Torpedo Tube Fiber Optic Interface Upgrade, SSBN TRIDENT Planned Equipment Replacement Program (TRIPER) Obsolescence, Low Pressure Electrolyzer (LPE) fielding and EDG
 Rotatable Pool program. Emergent EDG failures caused over 1600 lost days of OHIO Class Ao outside of availabilities from FY16-21. A rotatable pool of EDG parts will reduce risk to EDG Renewals within all
 SSBN ERPs.

 The FY 2026 request for Strategic Platform Support Equipment includes $38,039 thousand of discretionary and $15,701 thousand of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $53,740 thousand. The mandatory
 funds the OHIO Class HM&E Modernization efforts, Electric Hull Penetrators Procurement, Life Support System (LPE) Procurement, and partially funds SWSS Sustainability efforts in FY 2026. Further
 information for this reconciliation request is provided in Section 200010 (Readiness) of the Reconciliation Exhibit.

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $.604 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 Footnotes:
 (1)
   Development of SWSS VAMP MOD 3 solution is required to modernize the equipment to ensure the SWSS program continues to effectively function with the boat's Technical Insertion (TI) platform as it is upgraded by Submarine Warfare
 Federated Tactical System (SWFTS). If not modernized, the new SWSS equipment will not function properly.
 (2)
    The Strategic Weapons Support Systems (SWSS) sensors being replaced include MHC Water temp sensors, MHC Pump DP Sensor, DP Airflow Sensor, MDD Differential Pressure Sensors, MDD Gauge Pressure Sensors, MG Trap Tank
 Liquid Level Sensor, MHC Expansion Tank Liquid Level Sensor, and Electro-Pneumatic Transducer.

1810N / 01 / 9                                                                                 0950 / Strategic Platform Support Equip                                                        Strategic Platform Support Equip
 (3)
    Starting in FY24, funds provide solution development for underperforming systems or obsolete parts with no commercially available alternatives. This includes several known safety issues on OHIO Class submarines. Modernizations
 targeted in this line specifically address reliability and risk reduction on systems which have negatively impacted SSBN Ao.
 (4)
       Additional funding in FY26 funds the procurement and installation of Passive Fire Protection (PFP) materials (Promat microporous insulation wrap and NoFire A18-NV coating intumescent paint) on all SSBN missile tubes.
 (5)
    Following the USS MIAMI fire, a high-risk vulnerability assessment was conducted on the ability to detect and respond to a major fire, within OHIO Class strategic weapons system spaces, in a low manning condition (i.e. refit or overhaul).
 A permanently installed fire detection system was identified to partially mitigate this vulnerability by allowing for rapid recognition and response to a fire casualty as well as eliminate production/manning costs to establish/disestablish
 temporary systems during repair/modernization periods.
 (6)
    LPE unit orders are timed to support installations during a long refit. Installations are also aligned when current oxygen generators would receive required replacement of cells which are necessary for continued operation of legacy oxygen
 generators, thus avoiding an approximate $2M/ship additional maintenance cost. Funding supports procurement of Low Pressure Electrolyzers (LPEs) on OHIO Class submarines for two (2) procurements in FY24 and two (2) in FY26 to
 coincide with availability schedules. Install in FY24 supports previously procured in FY22 with an 18-month lead time. Each OHCL boat requires 2 LPEs. No LPE procurements planned in FY25.
 (7)
   Submarine Life Support Systems design and development supports ongoing efforts for Central Atmospheric Monitoring System (CAMS), Diesel Exhaust, Electro Oxygen Generators (EOG), Automatic Electro-Oxygen Generators (AEOG),
 Oxygen Concentration (SAROS), other oxygen generators (ILPE, AILPE, LPE), CO2 Scrubbers, and Burners.
 (8)
       OPNAV transferred Common Platform Engineering from Budget Line Item 2676 into 0950 commencing in FY25 as a zero-sum transfer of funds.
 (9)
       OPNAV transferred Combat Control System (CCS) Revision Eng/Cert Test from Budget Line Item 2676 into 0950 commencing in FY25 as a zero-sum transfer of funds.
 (10)
        OPNAV transferred SSBN Legacy CCS Modernization from Budget Line Item 2676 into 0950 commencing in FY25 as a zero-sum transfer of funds.
 (11)
    Funding commences in FY25 and provides for NRE, procurement, and installation of new torpedo tube penetrators in order to interface with the new MK48 Mod 8 / Mod 9 fiber optic guidance wire interface which replaces the legacy
 copper wire system.
 (12)
        Funding commences in FY25 for TRIDENT Planned Equipment Replacement (TRIPER) obsolescence solutions.
 (13)
    Funding commences in FY25 and provides for NRE and ALT development of multiple Emergency Diesel Generator (EDG) upgrades, procurement of installation material, to include structural/system modification to support installation. The
 FY25 to FY26 decrease is due to some development efforts maturing.
 (14)
     Funding commences in FY25 for Emergency Diesel Generator (EDG) rotatable pool to reduce risk to availabilities by ensuring long-lead time parts are kitted and available for execution. Increased funding in FY26 is required for additional
 long-lead time parts in order to fully standup the pool.
 (15)
    Funding commences in FY25 to include non-recurring engineering as well as initial buy of equipment needed to develop and test the new diesel engine control system. The FY25 to FY26 decrease is due to most development efforts
 maturing.
 (16)
        FY26 funding installs Life Support System (LPE) material procured in FY24.
 (17)
      Beginning in FY22, significant effort to drive down the backlog of necessary updates for Configuration Record changes, Liaison Action Request processing, Selected Drawings (SRD) mark-ups for SHIPALT-related work, accomplishment
 of Availability work packages (AWP) requirements assigned to the Planning Yard, such as Design Review hanger inspections, Integrated Logistic Support product updates, and Configuration Overhaul Planning Product development for each
 availability (ERP/ERO). This paperwork, which started to significantly backlog in the last few years, is vital to properly sustaining each Strategic boat by ensuring clear and accurate as-built configuration control of capabilities installed in each
 hull, as well as to execute expedient new development and incorporation of new technology onboard these Strategic platform.

 Description:
 The Undersea Special Mission Systems Program, formerly Deep Submergence Systems Program (DSSP), is responsible for the procurement, life cycle support, improvement and modernization of assigned
 platforms and programs. The Undersea Special Missions Systems Program provides for the procurement of equipment to support the establishment and maintenance of fleet capability for a number of programs
 which perform submarine rescue, escape, search, inspection, and object location and retrieval from the ocean environment. Submarine escape and rescue procurements replace obsolete, non-supportable
 equipment and subsystems through phased improvement and modernization projects. These projects may include special ship alterations, field change kits, and design corrections.

 FY2026 funding provides modernization of special mission systems to include the Navy's only Submarine Rescue System (SRS) assets. Modernization of SRS is required to address known reliability and
 obsolescence issues and support the planned minimum 15 year system service life extension prior to SRS's end of life and inoperability.


 SOURCES: The sources for these acquisitions are limited. There are few private companies actively engaged in deep ocean engineering and even fewer with the specialized certifications, experience,
 knowledge, and facilities to meet the exacting requirements of submarine escape and rescue. Accordingly, sole source contracts are sometimes required; however, where possible, contracting via open
 competition is utilized.

 [P40A / HJ030 - RESCUE SUPPORT EQUIPMENT]: INTERVENTION SYSTEMS (REMOTELY OPERATED VEHICLE (ROV)/SRS/SUBMARINE RESCUE CHAMBER (SRC) Intervention assets (ROV) are
 required to support the SRS and SRC world-wide rescue capability mission. Intervention assets are used to clear disabled submarine's seating surfaces, attach the SRC downhaul cable, attach salvage fittings,
 deliver emergency life support stores, and assess distressed submarines. The ROV is an unmanned system that reduces operational risk from the previous Intervention Assets that were a manned system,
 Atmospheric Diving System (ADS); transition from ADS to ROV has occurred and is in-service. SRC provides shallow water un-pressurized rescue capability. SRS is the deep water pressurized rescue ready
 system for the U.S. Navy. SRS was delivered in increments; the Submarine Rescue System-Rescue Capable System (SRS-RCS) completed OPEVAL in FY08, and the final component of SRS was the Transfer
 Under Pressure (TUP) or Submarine Rescue System-Submarine Decompression System (SRS-SDS) which has been delivered and completed Initial Operating Capability (IOC)/Final Operating Capability (FOC)
 in FY2019.

 Justification:
 FY 2026 funding is to continue the U.S. Submarine Rescue System (SRS) modernization and upgrade. Funding levels required in order to meet fleet wholeness requirements for resiliency related to submarine
 rescue system critical items, sparing, and operational availability and crucial field changes.

 FY2026 increase over FY2025 supports scheduled procurement of those technologies and subsystems developed in previous years that support the modernization of SRS, to include the procurement and
 implementation of condition-based maintenance into the system to increase operational availability. Navy added these additional resources to provide a credible SRS for our sailors and ally partner nations.

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $.007 million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 Description:
 The LCAC (Landing Craft, Air Cushion), which consists of both the legacy and LCAC 100 classes of craft, mission is to transport weapons systems, equipment, cargo, and personnel of the assault elements
 for the Marine Air/Ground Task Force from ship to shore and across the beach. The LCAC rides on a cushion of air contained in a flexible skirt and is propelled by two aft-mounted, reversible, variable pitch
 propellers. It is capable of speeds in excess of 40 knots. The LCAC sustainment funding is programmed for equipment procurement using OPN to replace selected engines, Personnel Transport Modules and
 other equipment that the fleet urgently requires to maintain acceptable levels of readiness.

 LC001 - LCAC SYSTEM UPGRADES
 The LCAC System Upgrade Program provides procurement and installation of components required to maintain acceptable levels of fleet operational readiness. LCAC System Upgrade Program will also provide
 materials to support systems upgrades. Equipment removal and installation will take place at Assault Craft Unit FOUR (ACU 4), ACU 5 and Naval Beach Unit SEVEN (NBU 7). Specific items procured vary from
 year to year based on Fleet needs; these items include but are not limited to C4N upgrades and deep skirt replacements.

 LC002 - ENGINES
 The ETF-40B engines are enhanced versions of the current TF-40B engines and are installed on Service Life Extension Program (SLEP) craft. Catastrophic engine failures have highlighted the need for spare
 engines to reduce operational downtime. Engine procurements are also for Pack Up Kits (PUKs) that accompany fleet deployment of LCACs aboard amphibious ships. Additional ETF-40B Engines will be needed
 to meet operational needs.

 LC007 - LIGHTWEIGHT ARMOR
 The portable lightweight armor system replaces the existing armor system. The portable system can be transported via a Container Express (CONEX) box and provides the fleet ballistic/fragmentation protection
 to manned spaces on the craft as well as to selected machinery components.

 LC008 - COLD WEATHER KITS
 LCAC requires to be capable of operating in temperature ranges from 10 degrees Fahrenheit to 100 degrees Fahrenheit. In order to operate in weather conditions below 50 degrees Fahrenheit, a cold weather
 kit is required. Snow and ice accumulation leads to actuation of a safety system that bypasses the main engine inlet air filtration system and allows engines to draw in unfiltered air. Cold weather kits prevent
 snow and ice accumulation in the main engines during cold weather operations keeping the craft operational during deployments.

1810N: Other Procurement, Navy / BA 01: Ships Support Equipment / BSA 9: Other 0970 / LCAC
Shipboard Equipment
ID Code (A=Service Ready, B=Not Service Ready): A                     Program Elements for Code B Items: N/A                                Other Related Program Elements: N/A
Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: N/A
 LC009 - PERSONNEL TRANSPORT MODULE
 The Personnel Transport Module (PTM) provides the capability of the Landing Craft Air Cushion (LCAC) to transport personnel and protect occupants from the elements inherent in an amphibious environment.

 LC010 - LCAC 100 CLASS MAJOR SHORE BASED SPARES (Ship to Shore Connector, SSC)
 Major Shore Based Spares (MSBS) Spares to be procured beginning in FY22 include major equipment with long lead times including such items as Engines, Skirts, Composites, Auxiliary Power Units, Propellers,
 Bow and Stern Ramps, Gearboxes, Lift Fan Impellers, and Fuel Pump & Metering Units. First craft arrived at the ACU 4 in FY22.

 LC011 - TRAINER SYSTEM UPGRADES AND MODERNIZATION
 Upgrade / modernization of the LCAC Full Motion Trainer (FMT), which provides essential training for the Operator, Engineer, Navigator, and Group Commander of the LCAC, in the complex skills required for the
 safe operation of the LCAC in both normal and casualty mode.

 Justification:

Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                               -           1.880           0.357          2.104             -            2.104             -               -         -            -         -               -
Flyaway Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                             -              -               -              -              -               -              -               -         -            -         -               -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                 -              -               -              -              -               -              -               -         -            -         -               -


 Description:
 Underwater Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD) program supplies EOD and maritime expeditionary forces with the necessary allowance outfitting, and diving and diving-related equipment aligned to fulfill
 assigned missions that includes Expeditionary Mine Countermeasures (ExMCM), and other maritime expeditionary missions. Most equipment must have inherently low acoustic and magnetic signatures to
 support maritime operations in the vicinity of influence fired ordnance. This funding supports outfitting the Navy's only comprehensive capability that can conduct full spectrum detect-to-engage ExMCM missions.
 Specifically, the (EOD) program supports EOD units worldwide as well as Mobile Diving and Salvage Units (MDSUs) and Naval Construction Forces (NCF) across the globe.

 The nature of the missions assigned to EOD and MCM forces under OPNAVINST 3501.97, Required Operational Capabilities & Projected Operational Environments (ROC/POE), require an aggregated set of
 systems and equipment to perform the wide range of mine countermeasures, underwater detection, location, investigation, force protection, exploitation, and neutralization tasks that comprise the mission set.
 The requirements for the procurement of each of these system suites are articulated in OPNAV approved requirements documents in accordance with DoD Directive 5000.2, SECNAVINST 5000.2, and the
 JCIDS process as defined by CJSCINST 3170.01 and defined by the JROC approved ExMCM ICD.

Note: Totals in this Exhibit P-40 set may not be exact or sum exactly due to rounding.

 Justification:
 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $0.374 million for civilian personnel to optimize the workforce in compliance with Executive Order 14210, "Implementing the President's Department of Government
 Efficiency Workforce Optimization Initiative."

 FY 2026 funding is required to continue procurement and fielding of Underwater EOD program systems and equipment to outfit ExMCM Companies, EOD Units, MDSUs, and NCFs with advanced sensors
 and hardware required to accomplish their OPNAV approved missions. This equipment will allow warfighters to detect, locate, neutralize, and render safe unexploded ordnance including improvised explosive
 devices; conduct expeditionary salvage; and underwater construction missions.

 FY 2026 funding continues procurement of Maritime Expeditionary Standoff Response (MESR) Payload Kits and MESR Platforms.

 -CE 4.1.3 MK16 MOD 1 Product Improvement Program (PIP) also known as the MK16 MOD 3: OPNAV N957 direction to upgrade the fielded inventory of MK16 MOD 1 UBAs to MOD 3 UBAs and procure and
 upgrade additional UBAs as necessary to meet the inventory objective of 468. Inventory objective will be met by converting the existing fleet inventory of 357 MK16 MOD 1s to MK16 MOD 3s and procurement of
 55 MK16 MOD 3 units FY 2026 and 56 units in FY 2027. An additional 52 spare conversion kits will also be procured.

 Additionally, this line enables production engineering and integrated logistics support needed for all ExMCM Companies, EOD Units, MDSUs, and NCFs. These systems provide the only ExMCM capability in the
 US Navy. If not properly outfitted, these EOD forces will not be able to detect, locate, neutralize, and render safe underwater ordnance, sea mines, and other explosive hazards, which will significantly degrade
 Fleet Maneuver against near peer adversaries. Specifically, this procures the next generation of Advanced Diver Integrated Sensors (STRIDENT), significantly increasing EOD diver capability to neutralize,
 destroy, or recover undersea mines.

 The below systems show an increase from FY25 to FY26 procurement quantities to meet validated Fleet inventory objectives, as outlined in approved buy plans.

 -CE 2.1.2 ADVANCED DIVER INTEGRATION SENSORS
 -CE 2.1.6 MESR PAYLOAD KITS Sensors
 -CE 2.1.7 MESR PAYLOAD KITS Kinetic Effects
 -CE 2.1.8 MESR PAYLOAD KITS C3 Modules
 -CE 2.1.9 MESR Platforms
